overview: Al Iman College's Tier 1 scholarship recognizes exceptional academic achievement with a 100% tuition fee discount. This merit-based program rewards students who achieve an average of 95% or above across all subjects in both semesters.
description: The Tier 1 scholarship is part of Al Iman College's tiered annual scholarship program designed to recognize academic excellence. Students achieving an average of 95% or above across all subjects for both semesters are eligible for a 100% discount on tuition fees. The scholarship is applicable to Year 5 and above students who maintain a minimum of 90% attendance throughout the year and have not received any orange or red cards. The discount is applied quarterly to tuition fees in the following year after all criteria are met.
eligibility_criteria: Year 5 and above; Average grades of 95% and above for both semesters across all subjects; Minimum 90% attendance throughout the year; No orange or red cards received during the year.
special_conditions: Minimum 90% attendance required; No disciplinary cards (orange or red) allowed

overview: Al Iman College's Tier 2 scholarship recognizes strong academic achievement with a 50% tuition fee discount. This program rewards students who maintain an average between 90-94% across all subjects while meeting attendance and conduct requirements.
description: The Tier 2 scholarship is part of Al Iman College's tiered annual scholarship program designed to recognize solid academic performance. Students achieving an average between 90% to 94% across all subjects for both semesters are eligible for a 50% discount on tuition fees. This scholarship applies to Year 5 and above students who maintain a minimum of 90% attendance throughout the year and have not received any orange or red cards. The discount is applied quarterly to tuition fees in the following year after all criteria are met.
eligibility_criteria: Year 5 and above; Average grades between 90-94% for both semesters across all subjects; Minimum 90% attendance throughout the year; No orange or red cards received during the year.
special_conditions: Minimum 90% attendance required; No disciplinary cards (orange or red) allowed

overview: The Select Entry Enrichment Program (SEEP) is Al-Taqwa College's premier scholarship offering 100% tuition coverage for high-ability students. The program provides full financial support to eligible students throughout their remaining schooling and VCE years, conditional on maintaining exceptional academic performance.
description: SEEP is designed to identify and support high-ability students with strong maturity and commitment to independent learning. Eligible students receive 100% scholarship coverage for the remainder of their schooling, including FREE tuition support throughout VCE. Additionally, successful students receive a $2,000 reward at the completion of Year 12, which can be applied towards university fees, a holiday, or Umrah. The program focuses on enrichment in English, Mathematics, and Science. Students must maintain a score of 90% or above in all assessments throughout the year to retain their scholarship status.
eligibility_criteria: Students with high level of maturity and commitment, capable of independent learning. Students in Grades 3 to Year 9 are eligible to apply each year. Students can be nominated by teachers. Grades 3-4 students are assessed internally. Students in Years 5-8 must sit the ACER High Ability Selection Test (HAST) during Term 3 to be eligible for scholarships for the following year. Current SEEP participants must also sit HAST to assess eligibility to remain in the program.
year_levels: Grade 3, Grade 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9
special_conditions: Students must maintain 90% or above in all assessments throughout the year to retain scholarship. Successful applicants will be sent offers by mail. Students intending to enrol must still follow the general enrolment process.

overview: SEEP is Al-Taqwa College's flagship merit-based scholarship program for high-achieving students. The program offers 100% tuition scholarships to eligible students in Grades 3 to Year 9, with additional benefits including free VCE support and university rewards. Students undertake enriched coursework in English, Mathematics and Science.
description: The Select Entry Enrichment Program is designed to identify and support highly capable students demonstrating high maturity, commitment, and independent learning ability. Eligible students receive a 100% scholarship for the remainder of their schooling, provided they maintain a score of 90% or above in all their assessments throughout the year. Recipients also receive free tuition support throughout VCE and a $2000 reward towards university fees, a holiday or Umrah upon successful completion of Year 12 studies. The program has enriched curriculum in English, Mathematics and Science to challenge high-ability learners. Successful applicants are notified by mail and must still follow the general college enrolment process.
eligibility_criteria: Students currently in Grades 3-4 are assessed internally. Students in Years 5-8 are eligible based on ACER High Ability Selection Test (HAST) results taken in Term 3. Current SEEP students must sit the HAST Test annually to maintain program eligibility. Teachers nominate students with high maturity, commitment and capacity for independent learning. The program is offered to students in Grades 3 to Year 9 each year.
year_levels: Grade 3, Grade 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9
value_aud: Full Fee Remission + Free VCE Support + $2,000 University Award
special_conditions: Students must maintain 90% or above in all assessments annually to retain scholarship. Must maintain high level of maturity and independent learning commitment throughout program.

overview: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students who are committed to their studies and strive for excellence. They are based on a student's ability to thrive academically, while contributing to school life in a positive and enthusiastic way. Recipients receive a concession of 25% or 50% off academic tuition fees.
description: Academic Scholarships recognise students demonstrating strong academic performance and commitment to excellence. These scholarships are available to both external applicants and current All Saints students. Scholarship awards are determined based on the candidate's performance in the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER) Scholarship Test, their resume and an interview with the School Principal or a delegate. All scholarships are subject to annual review based on academic performance and adherence to school values.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Years 6, 9, and 10 (in 2026) may apply for scholarships to commence in Years 7, 10 and 11 in 2027. Open to both external applicants and current All Saints students.
special_conditions: Annual review based on academic performance and adherence to school values. Information session held on Friday 23 January 2026.

overview: Music Scholarships are available for skilled instrumentalists or vocalists who bring passion and positivity to the arts at their current school. Recipients receive a concession of 25% or 50% off academic tuition fees plus free music lessons on one instrument.
description: Music Scholarships recognise talented musicians with demonstrated instrumental or vocal ability. In addition to fee concessions, Music Scholarship recipients receive free music lessons on one instrument as nominated by the Director of Performing Arts. Scholarships are available to both external applicants and current All Saints students. Scholarship awards are determined based on audition performance, their resume and an interview with the School Principal or a delegate.
eligibility_criteria: Skilled instrumentalists or vocalists. Music applicants are expected to be at least Grade 4 AMEB (or equivalent) for Year 7 entry and at least Grade 5 AMEB for Years 10 and 11. Students in Years 6, 9, and 10 (in 2026) may apply for scholarships to commence in Years 7, 10 and 11 in 2027.
value_aud: 25% or 50% Fee Reduction + Free Music Lessons
special_conditions: Minimum AMEB Grade 4 for Year 7, Grade 5 for Years 10-11. Annual review based on academic performance and adherence to school values.

overview: General Excellence Scholarships recognise students who display good academic performance, actively contribute to school life and excel in a particular area such as the arts, leadership, sport or community service. Recipients receive a concession of 25% or 50% off academic tuition fees.
description: General Excellence Scholarships are designed to recognise well-rounded students who demonstrate strong academic performance alongside excellence in other areas including arts, leadership, sport, or community service. These scholarships celebrate students who make positive contributions to school life while maintaining good academic standards. Scholarships are available to both external applicants and current All Saints students. Scholarship awards are determined based on the candidate's performance in the ACER Scholarship Test, their resume and an interview with the School Principal or a delegate.
eligibility_criteria: Students displaying good academic performance, who actively contribute to school life and excel in areas such as the arts, leadership, sport or community service. Students in Years 6, 9, and 10 (in 2026) may apply for scholarships to commence in Years 7, 10 and 11 in 2027.
special_conditions: Annual review based on academic performance and adherence to school values.

overview: Dedicated scholarship page with detailed information on Academic, Arts, Music, Sport, and Greek scholarships.

school_name: Australian International Academy of Education - Senior Campus
overview: The Australian International Academy of Education offers Academic Scholarships to both local and overseas students. The school has a total of 35 Full, Half, and Quarter Scholarships available across its Melbourne Senior Campus, King Khalid Coburg Campus and Caroline Springs Campus.
description: Academic Scholarships are merit-based awards designed to recognize and support academic excellence. The school offers Full, Half, and Quarter Scholarships to assist qualifying students with tuition fees. These scholarships are available to both local and international students. The programs aim to provide assistance with up to 100% of tuition fees depending on the scholarship level awarded.
eligibility_criteria: Open to both local and overseas students seeking admission to the school. Academic merit is the primary criterion for selection.
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction or 25% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarships available across Melbourne Senior Campus, King Khalid Coburg Campus (Primary), and Caroline Springs Campus

school_name: Australian International Academy of Education - Senior Campus
overview: The Australian International Academy of Education offers Quran Memorisation & Tajweed Scholarships to students demonstrating excellence in Quranic studies. This scholarship reflects the school's dedication to Islamic education within its curriculum.
description: These scholarships are specifically designed to recognize and support students who achieve excellence in Quran memorization and Tajweed (proper Quranic recitation). The school, which operates within a caring Islamic environment, offers these scholarships as part of its commitment to Islamic education. The awards aim to provide financial assistance to students who demonstrate exceptional achievement in these religious studies.
eligibility_criteria: Students must demonstrate achievement and excellence in Quran memorization and Tajweed. Open to local and overseas students seeking admission to the school.
special_conditions: Specific to students with achievement in Islamic Quranic studies

overview: Dedicated scholarship page with multiple scholarship programs including Academic, Performing Arts, Visual Arts, Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander, Excellence, and Regional and Rural scholarships.
Dedicated scholarships page with comprehensive information about academic, art, design, performing arts, boarding, and Indigenous scholarships.
Dedicated Senior School Scholarships page found with Sliding Doors Scholarship program details.
Dedicated scholarships and bursaries page with comprehensive details on academic, sports, music, and performing arts scholarships plus fee assistance.
Dedicated scholarship page found for senior school scholarships offering 50% tuition fee remission.
Dedicated scholarship page with multiple scholarship offerings including academic, music, general excellence, and means-tested scholarships.
Dedicated scholarship page with comprehensive information on Academic, Music/Academic, Principal's, Means-Tested, and Indigenous scholarships.
Dedicated scholarships page with multiple scholarship types including Academic, General Excellence, Access and Equity, and Humanitarian Scholarships.

school_name: Carey Baptist Grammar School - Donvale Campus
overview: Carey offers Academic Scholarships to recognise high academic achievement. These scholarships are available to students entering Years 7 and 10. Applicants must demonstrate excellence in academic subjects and sit an assessment examination conducted by Edutest.
description: Academic Scholarships at Carey Baptist Grammar School are merit-based awards designed to recognise and support students who demonstrate outstanding academic excellence. The scholarship covers a percentage of tuition fees and is renewable year to year provided the recipient maintains high academic standards. Successful applicants must perform consistently well academically and undergo a comprehensive selection process including online application, school report submission, and an academic test. The scholarship is tenable through Year 12 subject to maintaining the required academic standards.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 or Year 10. Applicants must demonstrate strong academic achievement in their current school reports. Must submit latest school report to Admissions Office. Must sit the Edutest examination. Shortlisted candidates will be invited for interview.
special_conditions: Late applications incur additional $150 fee. Supplementary exam service incurs additional $150 fee. Registration fee of $105 applies.

school_name: Carey Baptist Grammar School - Donvale Campus
overview: General Excellence Scholarships recognise outstanding achievement in sports, music, performing arts or other co-curricular activities. These scholarships are awarded to Year 7, 10 and 11 students who demonstrate excellence across their chosen field and maintain strong academic performance. The scholarship supports Carey's commitment to developing well-rounded students.
description: General Excellence Scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional talent and achievement in a range of co-curricular areas including sports and the arts. The scholarship usually covers 50% remission of tuition fees but does not cover application fees, books, uniforms, computers or consolidated fees. Shortlisted applicants are invited to interview with the Head of Sport and Activities, and undergo an academic test by Edutest. The Principal makes the final decision on scholarship awards. The scholarship is renewable annually and is tenable to Year 12 provided academic achievement, behaviour and co-curricular involvement meet expectations.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7, 10 or 11. Must demonstrate outstanding achievement in a recognised co-curricular activity (sports or performing arts). Eligible sports include Athletics, Basketball, Cricket, Cross Country, Football (AFL), Hockey, Netball, Rowing, Soccer, Softball, Swimming, Tennis and Volleyball. Must maintain consistent academic performance and good behaviour. Must contribute positively to school community and co-curricular activities.
special_conditions: Scholarship must be reviewed annually. Recipients must maintain academic performance and co-curricular participation. School reserves right to withdraw scholarship if conditions not met. Does not cover application/entry fees, books, uniforms, computers or consolidated fees.

school_name: Carey Baptist Grammar School - Donvale Campus
overview: Access and Equity Scholarships provide financial assistance to students experiencing financial disadvantage or hardship. These scholarships are designed to support Carey's commitment to social justice and increase diversity and inclusiveness within the student body. Awards are made based on a comprehensive financial assessment.
description: Access and Equity Scholarships at Carey Baptist Grammar School support students entering Year 7 or Year 10 who are experiencing financial disadvantage or hardship. The scholarship covers tuition fees from commencement in Year 7 or Year 10 through to and including Year 12. Successful applicants must be enthusiastic about attending school, likely to complete Year 12, and have demonstrated parental support. The school recognises that financial barriers should not prevent capable students from accessing a quality education and uses these scholarships to build a diverse and inclusive community. Applications are considered based on evidence of financial hardship subject to financial assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Students commencing in Year 7 or Year 10. Must be experiencing financial disadvantage or hardship. Must pass financial assessment demonstrating need. Must be enthusiastic about attending school. Must be likely to complete Year 12. Must have demonstrated parental/family support and encouragement for scholarship application and enrolment.
special_conditions: Subject to financial assessment. Applications close at end of Term 1 each year. Nominations can be made by school or parents. Principal discretion applies. School reserves right to review scholarship.

overview: Castlemaine Steiner School & Kindergarten offers partly or fully subsidised places to eligible applicants of new families who would otherwise not have the opportunity to enrol due to financial disadvantage. The scholarship seeks to foster an inclusive and diverse socioeconomic community and make Steiner education more available across the regional community.
description: The CSSK Scholarship provides a subsidy of up to 100 percent of tuition fees for eligible students of new families to the school for the duration of their enrolment at CSSK. Applications can be made at any time following a successful enrolment process. Scholarship applications are assessed based on financial disadvantage, commitment to Steiner education principles, and availability of school resources. Assessments are expected to be completed within four weeks of a received application.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be Australian citizens or permanent residents; have successfully completed the enrolment process at CSSK; provide evidence of financial disadvantage (generally met if the child is named as a card holder or dependent child on a valid government concession card such as Centrelink Health Care Card, Pensioner Concession Card, or Veterans Affairs Gold Card); and demonstrate commitment to the principles of Steiner education and CSSK.
special_conditions: Applications run year-round. Scholarship is for new families to the school only. Confidentiality of all applications is assured.

overview: Castlemaine Steiner School & Kindergarten offers Class 1 Inclusion Scholarships that are not contingent on financial background. These scholarships provide financial support to qualifying students entering Class 1.
description: The Class 1 Inclusion Scholarships are available for students entering Class 1. Unlike the general CSSK scholarship, this program is not dependent on financial disadvantage and may be based on other criteria such as educational merit or commitment to Steiner education principles. The specific details and conditions of this scholarship should be confirmed directly with the school's Enrolments Officer.
eligibility_criteria: Available for students entering Class 1. Not contingent on financial background. Contact the school for specific eligibility criteria.
special_conditions: Not dependent on financial background. Contact school for full details.

overview: Castlemaine Steiner School & Kindergarten operates a Fee Assistance Scheme to help provide short-term assistance for temporary periods of hardship. This scheme is designed to support families experiencing severe financial difficulty in meeting fee payments.
description: The Fee Assistance Scheme is available to help families experiencing temporary periods of financial hardship. Assistance is by application and dependent on available school funds. The scheme is intended for families with severe financial difficulty in meeting fee payments. Families receiving assistance are still required to pay the majority of the fees whilst receiving assistance. This is distinct from the scholarship program and is meant to address temporary hardship situations rather than ongoing financial disadvantage.
eligibility_criteria: Families with severe financial difficulty in meeting fee payments. Assistance is dependent on available school funds and must be applied for.
special_conditions: For temporary periods of hardship only. Families must still pay the majority of fees. Dependent on available school funds.

overview: Castlemaine Steiner School & Kindergarten operates a tax-deductible Bursary Fund that receives donations from the school community. These funds help support families in financial need.
description: The school operates a tax-deductible Bursary Fund which receives voluntary donations throughout the school year. These donations enable the bursary programs to develop and provide support to families requiring financial assistance. Donations can be made on a regular basis and are welcomed at any time throughout the school year.
eligibility_criteria: Access to bursary funds is available to eligible families as determined by the school.
special_conditions: Donations are tax-deductible. Funds are used to support families in financial need.

overview: Dedicated scholarship page with comprehensive details on multiple scholarship types including Academic, Music, Sport, Theatre, Visual Arts, Dance, and Foundation scholarships.
School has a dedicated scholarship and bursaries page offering academic scholarships for Year 7 and other financial assistance programs.

school_name: Christian College Geelong - Middle School – Highton
overview: Dedicated scholarships page with Academic Excellence, Music Excellence, and Community Access scholarship programs.

Cobram AGS has introduced a new scholarship program to recognise and reward the academic achievements of existing students as they progress through the school. The scholarship provides a 50% remission on tuition fees for the following year to students with the best academic record.
description: Students with the best academic record from Year 6 through to Year 11 will be awarded a 50% remission on tuition fees for the following year. These annual scholarships are a significant reward for high achieving students and will be formally recognised at the Presentation Day ceremony that concludes the academic year.
eligibility_criteria: Existing students in Year 6 through Year 11 with the best academic record during the current year.
year_levels: Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Annual scholarship awarded based on academic performance during the year; recognised at school Presentation Day ceremony.

overview: Cobram AGS Bursaries have been established to provide educational opportunities for students who have the potential to lead within the community but are facing financial hardship. These programs aim to make quality education accessible to students with demonstrated need.
description: Cobram AGS Bursaries provide educational opportunities for students who demonstrate leadership potential within the community but face financial hardship. The bursary program is designed to ensure that financial constraints do not prevent capable students from accessing the school's educational programs and community engagement opportunities.
eligibility_criteria: Students who demonstrate potential to lead within the community and are facing financial hardship.
special_conditions: Applicants must demonstrate leadership potential and financial hardship; contact Business Manager for application details.

overview: Dedicated scholarships page found with comprehensive information about scholarship programs including academic, creative, leadership, music, and service categories.

The Academic Pathway supports students who demonstrate strong academic potential, intellectual curiosity, and a commitment to learning. The Elevate Pathways Program provides enrichment experiences, mentorship and opportunities to extend learning beyond traditional scholarships.
description: The Academic Pathway is part of the Elevate Pathways Program, designed for students demonstrating strong academic potential and intellectual curiosity. Successful participants receive a tuition fee reduction along with mentoring and academic guidance. Participants access enrichment opportunities to extend learning, early VCE opportunities where appropriate, and support for relevant study. The program recognises potential early and provides a framework to nurture it deliberately through mentorship and structured extension.
eligibility_criteria: Domestic students (Australian citizens or permanent residents) entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027. Applications open to both current ELTHAM students and new applicants.
special_conditions: Requires submission of academic evidence and personal information. Selection includes review and shortlisting, interview with shortlisted applicants.

overview: The Arts Pathway supports students who are deeply committed to artistic disciplines and demonstrating strong potential. Part of the Elevate Pathways Program offering enrichment experiences, mentorship and opportunities to extend learning.
description: The Arts Pathway is designed for students demonstrating commitment to artistic pursuits and keen to develop within a holistic school environment. Successful participants receive mentoring and performance guidance along with a tuition fee reduction. The pathway includes training and development opportunities aligned to artistic interests, support for relevant artistic opportunities and experiences, and structured enrichment through mentorship.
eligibility_criteria: Domestic students (Australian citizens or permanent residents) entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027. Applications open to both current ELTHAM students and new applicants.
special_conditions: Selection based on demonstrated artistic talent and commitment to a discipline. Requires submission of artistic evidence and personal information.

overview: The Sport Pathway supports students committed to their sport and keen to develop as athletes within a holistic school environment. Participants are part of the College's Elite and Emerging Athletes Program (EEAP) receiving mentoring, training support and a tuition fee reduction.
description: The Sport Pathway is designed for students demonstrating commitment to athletic pursuits at a high level. Successful participants access mentoring and performance guidance, training and development opportunities aligned to their sport, support for relevant sporting opportunities and qualifications or experiences, and a tuition fee reduction. The program emphasises development as athletes within a holistic school environment where participation is supportive, not burdensome.
eligibility_criteria: Domestic students (Australian citizens or permanent residents) entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027. Applications open to both current ELTHAM students and new applicants. Students must demonstrate commitment to sport.
special_conditions: Students are part of the Elite and Emerging Athletes Program (EEAP). Participation is supportive, not pressured. Selection based on athletic potential and commitment to sport.

overview: Dedicated scholarship page with comprehensive information on Academic Excellence, Music, and All-Rounder scholarships.
Dedicated scholarship page with comprehensive information on academic, music, and sporting scholarships available for Years 7-12 students.
Dedicated scholarships and bursaries page found with comprehensive information on academic, basketball, music, and general excellence scholarships.
Dedicated scholarship page with multiple scholarship categories (Academic, General Excellence, Boarding, Music, Sport) and detailed eligibility criteria.

school_name: Geelong Lutheran College - Armstrong Creek Campus
overview: Geelong Lutheran College offers Academic Scholarships for Year 7 entry, recognising high academic achievement. The scholarship tenure extends from Year 7 to Year 12. Scholarships are awarded based on performance in the Edutest Scholarship Examination.
description: Academic Scholarships are available to existing and prospective students who demonstrate high academic achievement and are willing to embrace the ethos of the College. Scholarships provide up to half remission of tuition fees. The scholarship is tenure-based, running from Year 7 through to Year 12. Scholarships are reviewed each semester by the Head of Secondary and remain subject to continued excellence in academic performance and adherence to College policies and procedures. Scholarships apply to tuition fees only and do not apply to levies or other costs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and prospective students entering Year 7. Candidates must be Australian citizens or permanent residents. All scholarship candidates not already enrolled at the College must complete an enrolment application at the College prior to registering for the Scholarship Examination.
special_conditions: Scholarship is reviewed each semester and subject to continued academic excellence and adherence to College policies. Limited number available.

school_name: Geelong Lutheran College - Armstrong Creek Campus
overview: Geelong Lutheran College offers Academic Scholarships for Year 10 entry, recognising high academic achievement. The scholarship tenure extends from Year 10 to Year 12. Scholarships are awarded based on performance in the Edutest Scholarship Examination.
description: Academic Scholarships are available to existing and prospective students who demonstrate high academic achievement and are willing to embrace the ethos of the College. Scholarships provide up to half remission of tuition fees. The scholarship tenure runs from Year 10 through to Year 12. Scholarships are reviewed each semester by the Head of Secondary and remain subject to continued excellence in academic performance and adherence to College policies and procedures. Scholarships apply to tuition fees only and do not apply to levies or other costs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and prospective students entering Year 10. Candidates must be Australian citizens or permanent residents. All scholarship candidates not already enrolled at the College must complete an enrolment application at the College prior to registering for the Scholarship Examination.
special_conditions: Scholarship is reviewed each semester and subject to continued academic excellence and adherence to College policies. Limited number available.

school_name: Geelong Lutheran College - Armstrong Creek Campus
overview: The Geelong Lutheran College General Excellence Scholarships recognise achievement in Christian faith, leadership and community service, or talent in music, sport, performing arts and visual arts. Open to students entering Years 7 through 10, these scholarships celebrate all-round excellence beyond academics.
description: The General Excellence Scholarships acknowledge achievement in the areas of Christian faith, leadership and community service, or talent in music, sport, performing arts or visual arts. These scholarships recognise students who are dedicated, motivated and engaged in a variety of experiences within school and community life. The program values holistic development and contribution to the College community. Applications are open to both external students with an application for 2027 enrolment and currently enrolled Geelong Lutheran College students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9 or 10 at GLC. Includes external students with an application for 2027 enrolment in the system and currently enrolled Geelong Lutheran College students. Applicants should demonstrate achievement in Christian faith, leadership and community service, or talent in music, sport, performing arts or visual arts.
special_conditions: Open to Years 7, 8, 9 and 10 entry. Both external applicants and current students eligible.

overview: Dedicated scholarship page with Academic, Music, Sport and Foundation Scholarship details for Years 7-11.

The Year 7 Academic Scholarship is available for students entering Year 7 following completion of the ACER scholarship test during their final year of primary school. This scholarship recognises academic excellence and provides fee reductions to eligible recipients.
description: This scholarship is offered to students demonstrating strong academic potential for entry into Year 7. Recipients receive either 50% or 100% reduction in school fees over a two-year duration. The scholarship is valued at approximately $7,200 at 50% fee reduction and $14,400 at 100% fee reduction. Recipients who choose to leave the school during the term of their scholarship are required to pay back the value of the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: The candidate must be a Year 7 student in the year following the completion of the scholarship test. The scholarship applies for a two-year duration and provides either 50% or 100% reduction in school fees.
special_conditions: Recipients currently holding other scholarships cannot hold multiple scholarships simultaneously. Early entry (Year 8) also possible.

overview: The Year 9-12 Academic Scholarship is available for students entering Year 9 following completion of the ACER scholarship test in their second year of secondary school. This scholarship recognises academic merit and can be held for either two or four years.
description: This scholarship is offered to students demonstrating strong academic achievement for entry into Year 9. Recipients receive either 50% or 100% reduction in school fees over either a two-year or four-year duration. The two-year scholarship is worth approximately $7,200 at 50% and $14,400 at 100%. The four-year scholarship is worth approximately $14,400 at 50% and $28,000 at 100%. Recipients who choose to leave the school during the term of their scholarship are required to pay back the value of the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: The candidate must be a Year 9 student in the year following the completion of the scholarship test. The scholarship applies for either a two-year or four-year duration and provides either 50% or 100% reduction in school fees.
special_conditions: Recipients currently holding other scholarships cannot hold multiple scholarships simultaneously. Early entry (Year 10) also possible. Two-year or four-year duration options available.

overview: The VCE Academic Scholarship is available for students entering Year 11 following completion of the ACER scholarship test in Year 10. This scholarship recognises academic excellence at senior secondary level.
description: This scholarship is offered to students demonstrating strong academic potential for entry into Year 11 and the VCE program. Specific fee reduction amounts and duration details follow the same structure as other academic scholarships at the College. Recipients must maintain satisfactory academic progress throughout their VCE studies. Recipients who choose to leave the school during the term of their scholarship are required to pay back the value of the scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: The candidate must be a Year 11 student in the year following the completion of the scholarship test during Year 10.
special_conditions: Recipients currently holding other scholarships cannot hold multiple scholarships simultaneously.

overview: The Principal's Scholarship is allocated at the discretion of the Principal to students or prospective students who do not qualify for existing scholarship opportunities but are deemed worthy contenders for recognition.
description: These scholarships are awarded by the Principal to recognise exceptional merit in students who may not meet the criteria for standard academic scholarships. The scholarship acknowledges overall excellence, character, and contribution to the College community. The Principal may award scholarships based on various criteria including academic achievement, leadership, service, or other exceptional qualities demonstrated by the student.
eligibility_criteria: Candidates must be students or prospective students of Gilson College who do not qualify for one of the existing scholarship opportunities but in the opinion of the Principal are worthy contenders for recognition.
special_conditions: Current recipients of scholarships are eligible for only one scholarship at any given time. No overlapping scholarships are permitted. Award determined by Principal discretion.

overview: Dedicated scholarships page found with multiple scholarship programs available.
Dedicated scholarship page with multiple scholarship programs including Social Justice Scholarships and Priority Entry Programmes.
Haileybury has a dedicated scholarships page offering multiple scholarship types including academic, general excellence, indigenous, music and socio-economic support.

Academic scholarships awarded to students who demonstrate outstanding academic performance and commitment to their studies. Available for entry into Years 5, 7 and 10. Awards are partial scholarships only, tenable until Year 12.
description: Haileybury Academic Scholarships are awarded to students who exhibit outstanding academic performance and commitment to their studies. Students are required to complete academic testing administered by Edutest, covering Literacy, Numeracy and Comprehension. Shortlisted students may be required to complete second-round testing and interviews with Head of Campus. Scholarships are awarded from year of entry through until Year 12, with continuation assessed annually against scholarship acceptance terms and conditions.
eligibility_criteria: Students must currently be enrolled in Year 3, 5 or 8 (for entry in 2028). Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident of Australia. Year 3 students currently attending Haileybury are ineligible to apply.
special_conditions: Scholarship continuation assessed annually; partial scholarships only; testing administered by Edutest; Year 3 currently attending Haileybury ineligible

overview: Excellence scholarships awarded to students demonstrating outstanding achievement and contribution across academics, leadership, community service, sport and the arts. Available for entry into Years 5, 7 and 10 in 2027.
description: Haileybury Excellence Scholarships recognise outstanding achievement and contribution not only in academics but also in leadership, community service, sport and the arts, both within and beyond the school environment. Scholarships are available at Berwick, Brighton, City and Keysborough campuses. The Scholarships Committee reviews all applications to determine suitable students who will benefit from a Haileybury education and contribute to the Haileybury community. Shortlisted students and families are invited to interview with Head of School. Indigenous scholarships are also available under this category, open to talented students of Australian Indigenous descent.
eligibility_criteria: Students must be currently enrolled in Year 4, 6 or 9 (for 2027 entry). Must be Australian citizen or resident. Indigenous applicants must provide confirmation of Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander heritage.
special_conditions: Indigenous scholarships available within this category; scholarship remission determined by Scholarships Committee; no feedback provided to unsuccessful applicants

overview: Means-tested scholarships providing significant financial assistance to deserving students who would not ordinarily be able to attend Haileybury. Awards between 50-100% remission of tuition fees for the duration of schooling at Haileybury.
description: Haileybury Foundation Scholarships provide the opportunity for students to benefit from a Haileybury education. Scholarships may be awarded to new students or to current students whose financial circumstances have changed. These scholarships are generously provided by Old Haileyburians, parents, staff and friends. The foundation offers between 50-100% remission of tuition fees for duration of schooling. Recipients gain access to Haileybury's renowned academic curriculum, co-curricular activities and comprehensive wellbeing programs, plus a welcoming sense of belonging within the Haileybury community with opportunities to connect across Haileybury's campuses and alumni network.
eligibility_criteria: Victorian students transitioning into Year 7, 9 or 10 at any Victorian campus. Must not ordinarily be able to attend Haileybury based on current financial situation. Must be positive contributors to the community. Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident within the meaning of the Australian Citizenship Act 1948. Must satisfy equity requirements of Rules of Haileybury Scholarship Fund.
special_conditions: Means-tested; requires financial documentation and academic reports; interview required with Senior Executive and Foundation Board member; must be Victorian resident

overview: Dedicated scholarship page with multiple scholarship types including academic, foundation, and means-tested scholarships.

Access Awards are means-tested financial assistance programs designed to help families who would not otherwise have the financial means to afford a CREST (Hillcrest/Rivercrest) Christian College education. The awards provide significant tuition fee reductions to eligible families.
description: Access Awards offer means-tested financial assistance to families of Hillcrest Christian College students. It is CREST's hope that all families have access to leading, Christian education. For this reason, CREST introduces Access Awards to families who would not otherwise have the financial means to afford a CREST education. Eligible families can receive up to 50% off tuition fees until the completion of Year 12. Access Awards are available to students who are already attending, students who are enrolled at CREST Colleges (who have not yet commenced), and to new students.
eligibility_criteria: Years 9-12 students (current, enrolled, or new students). Applicants must be shortlisted and provide evidence of family financial circumstances as evidenced in income tax statements. Students who are not enrolled must complete an Expression of Interest or Enrolment Application Form prior to submitting an application for Access Awards.
special_conditions: Means-tested award requiring financial documentation (income tax statements). Period of Award: Until Year 12 is completed.

overview: Dedicated scholarship page with comprehensive information about Year 7, Year 10, and VCE scholarship programs.

Hume Anglican Grammar's Year 7 Academic Scholarship is designed for eligible students entering Year 7. The scholarship recognises academic excellence and potential through a comprehensive examination administered by EduTest. Scholarships are offered as a percentage-based fee remission.
description: The Year 7 Academic Scholarship is part of Hume Anglican Grammar's Academic Scholarship Program. Students who demonstrate strong academic performance and potential are considered for scholarships that provide fee remission for the duration of their secondary schooling at the school. The scholarship value ranges as a percentage of school tuition fees depending on the strength of the application and is at the discretion of the Principal. The scholarship excludes uniforms, books and other items listed in the Annual Fee Schedule. Successful applicants must maintain expected levels of performance and complete their secondary education at the school, or parents/guardians will be required to repay the scholarship value.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be entering Year 7 in 2029 (for the February 2027 exam), Year 9 in 2028, or Year 10 in 2028. Applicants must be eligible for consideration for enrolment at Hume Anglican Grammar. International students (including those holding Visa Subclass 500 or 600) cannot be considered for enrolment. Applications open in Term 4 of the year when the applicant is in Year 4 for Year 7 entry.
special_conditions: Scholarship must be accepted within stipulated time. Students must maintain expected levels of performance and complete secondary education at the school. Repayment required if student leaves prior to completing Year 12.

overview: The VCE Scholarship at Hume Anglican Grammar is designed for students entering Year 11 and Year 12. It recognises strong academic performance and potential to thrive in the school's high-quality learning environment. Scholarships provide up to 80% fee remission for VCE years.
description: The VCE Scholarship is available for current and external Year 10 or Year 11 students entering Year 11 or Year 12. The scholarship provides fee remission as a percentage of school tuition fees for the VCE years of schooling (Years 11 and 12) with a maximum remission of 80%, at the discretion of the Principal. The scholarship value ranges as a percentage depending on the strength of the application. The scholarship excludes uniforms, books and other excluded items listed in the Annual Fee Schedule. Applications require submission of two most recent school reports and NAPLAN summaries, and shortlisted candidates are contacted for interview.
eligibility_criteria: Current and external Year 10 or Year 11 students entering Year 11 or Year 12 in 2027 are eligible. Applicants must demonstrate overall academic performance and potential to thrive in the school's learning environment, achievement and involvement in other areas of excellence, demonstrated leadership and teamwork capabilities, and commitment to Hume Anglican Grammar School Values. External applicants must also complete an Application for Enrolment if not already done.
special_conditions: Applications for 2027 will open later in 2026. Only shortlisted candidates are contacted for interview. Successful candidates must accept within stipulated time and maintain expected performance levels. Repayment required if student leaves prior to completing Year 12.

overview: Academic scholarships are awarded to high-achieving students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and potential at Huntingtower. These scholarships, offered in conjunction with ACER, cover 25-50% of tuition fees for entry into Years 7, 9, and 10.
description: Academic scholarships are designed to recognize and support students who excel academically and who will also contribute to the school in many other ways. Candidates must be high achieving students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and who are predicted to continue to do so at Huntingtower. The scholarship value covers 25-50% of tuition fees. A limited number of scholarships are awarded based on examination results, portfolio review, and interview conducted at the Principal's discretion.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7, 9, or 10. Must sit ACER Academic Examination at Huntingtower on the set date. Must be high-achieving students with exceptional academic ability. All applicants must submit an electronic scholarship portfolio including school reports (latest full year including Semester 1 and 2), certificates of achievement, and evidence of sporting, music, leadership, or community service achievements.
special_conditions: Candidates must sit ACER exam on set date to be eligible. Schools do not share scholarship results with one another. Scholarships awarded at sole discretion of Principal.

overview: General Excellence scholarships recognize well-rounded students who achieve high academic standards and excel in at least two co-curricular areas. These scholarships cover 25-50% of tuition fees and are available for entry into Years 7, 9, and 10.
description: General Excellence scholarships are awarded to students who have achieved a high academic standard, plus excellence in at least two of the following areas: sport, music, leadership, or community service. Designed for well-rounded students who demonstrate achievements across academic and co-curricular domains, these scholarships cover 25-50% of tuition fees. A limited number of scholarships are awarded based on examination results, portfolio review, and interview at the Principal's sole discretion.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7, 9, or 10. Must have achieved a high academic standard. Must demonstrate excellence in at least two of the following areas: sport, music, leadership, or community service. Must sit ACER Academic Examination at Huntingtower on the set date. All applicants must submit an electronic scholarship portfolio with school reports, certificates of achievement, and evidence of excellence in relevant co-curricular areas.
special_conditions: Must demonstrate excellence in at least two co-curricular areas (sport, music, leadership, or community service). Candidates must sit ACER exam on set date. Scholarships awarded at sole discretion of Principal.

overview: Music scholarships are awarded to students demonstrating exceptional musical ability and potential. Available for entry into Years 7, 9, and 10, these scholarships cover 25-50% of tuition fees and require both audition and academic examination.
description: Music scholarships recognize students with exceptional talent and potential in music performance. Applicants must be skilled on eligible instruments (all instruments except voice) and demonstrate high musical ability. Pianoforte players must meet minimum AMEB grade levels. The scholarship covers 25-50% of tuition fees. A limited number of scholarships are awarded based on ACER Academic Examination results, portfolio review, and live music audition or video audition submission. Recipients are expected to actively participate in the school's music program and contribute to the musical life of the school.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7, 9, or 10. Must have achieved a high academic standard. All instruments eligible except voice. Pianoforte players must meet minimum AMEB level. Must sit ACER Academic Examination at Huntingtower on the set date. Must submit a video music audition (2-3 minutes) or attend live audition on scheduled date. Electronic scholarship portfolio required including school reports, certificates of achievement, and evidence of music ability/achievements.
special_conditions: Video audition (2-3 minutes unedited, with or without accompaniment, 2 contrasting works) due by 11:59pm 3 February 2026. Live auditions scheduled for 15 February 2026 (times advised after close date). All instruments eligible except voice. Pianoforte players require minimum AMEB level. Applicants must sit ACER exam on set date.

overview: Dedicated scholarship page with detailed information about scholarships for years 7-12.
Dedicated scholarships page found on official school website.

Ivanhoe Girls' Grammar School offers Academic Scholarships to Year 5 students commencing in 2027. These scholarships are awarded based on academic merit and recipients must embody the school values of Kindness, Connectedness, Curiosity, Vibrancy, and Bravery.
description: The Academic Scholarship for Year 5 entry is designed to recognise academic excellence and the values alignment of the school community. The scholarship covers the final two years of Junior School (Years 5-6) and the full six years of Senior School (Years 7-12) at Ivanhoe Girls' Grammar School. Recipients must maintain continued academic progress, active involvement in school life, and upholding of school values throughout their tenure. All candidates are required to sit an academic scholarship examination administered by Academic Assessment Services and will be assessed holistically based on written applications, past school reports, and examination results.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be in Year 4 in 2026 to be eligible. Open to domestic candidates only. Preference given to students and families who have actively engaged with the school and have made it their school of choice.
special_conditions: Shortlisted candidates invited to interview with Principal or Head of Junior School. Scholarship contingent on continued academic progress and active school involvement.

overview: The School offers Academic Scholarships to students commencing Year 7 in 2027, awarded on the basis of academic excellence. Recipients embody the school values of Kindness, Connectedness, Curiosity, Vibrancy, and Bravery.
description: The Academic Scholarship for Year 7 entry recognises academic excellence and character alignment with school values. The scholarship covers the full six years of secondary education (Years 7-12) at Ivanhoe Girls' Grammar School, contingent upon continued academic progress, active involvement in school life, and upholding of school values. All candidates are assessed holistically, with consideration given to written applications, past school reports, and results in the academic scholarship examination administered by Academic Assessment Services. Shortlisted candidates are invited to interview with the Principal or Head of Senior School.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be in Year 6 in 2026 to be eligible. Open to domestic candidates only. Preference given to students and families who have actively engaged with the school and identified it as their school of choice.
special_conditions: Shortlisted candidates invited to interview with Principal or Head of Senior School. Scholarship contingent on continued academic progress and active school involvement.

overview: Students entering Years 10 or 11 in 2027 are invited to apply for the High Achievers Scholarship. This scholarship is not available to current students at Ivanhoe Girls' Grammar School and recognises academic achievement, co-curricular involvement, and leadership.
description: The High Achievers Scholarship is designed for high-achieving students entering Years 10 and 11 from external schools. Applicants are assessed on three main criteria: Academic Achievement (strong record of academic performance with potential to excel in VCE); Co-curricular Involvement (engagement in performing and visual arts, music, sport, debating, and extracurricular pursuits); and Leadership and Collaboration (evidence of leadership skills and teamwork). All candidates must sit an academic scholarship examination administered by Academic Assessment Services. Scholarships awarded cover partial remission of tuition fees until the completion of Year 12, provided satisfactory progress is maintained.
eligibility_criteria: Open to external students entering Years 10 or 11 in 2027. Not available to current Ivanhoe Girls' Grammar School students. Demonstrated strong academic performance with potential to excel in VCE. Evidence of co-curricular involvement and leadership skills.
special_conditions: Not available to current students. Assessed on academic achievement, co-curricular involvement, and leadership criteria. Contingent on satisfactory progress in Year 12.

overview: Dedicated scholarship page with multiple scholarship programs including Academic, High Achievers, and General Excellence Awards.
Kardinia International College offers Music Scholarships to students entering Years 7-11 based on demonstrated musical excellence and achievement at recommended AMEB grade levels or higher.
description: Music scholarships are awarded to applicants on the basis of a video audition, a shortlisted live audition, school reports and an interview with a member of the College Leadership Team. Successful students who are shortlisted to attend an in-person audition will perform two works of contrasting styles, complete simple aural tests, a short sight-reading test and conclude with a discussion outlining their musical interests and goals. The audition and interview take approximately 20 minutes. Students are welcome to audition on more than one instrument if they have reached the suggested levels on each instrument.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7-11 must have an application for enrolment at the College. Students should be playing at a recommended AMEB grade level or higher for their year level; lower levels are acceptable for brass and vocal students. All applicants must be enrolled or seeking enrolment at the College.
special_conditions: Instrumentalists should provide their own accompanist or backing CD for accompanied works. Video audition is required as first stage of selection.

overview: From 2027, Kardinia International College offers Bridges to the Future Scholarships to students entering Years 7-11 who demonstrate general excellence and could not otherwise attend the College for financial reasons. The scholarship recognises academic achievement combined with high achievement in arts, sport, leadership, science and technology, community service or social justice.
description: The Bridges to the Future Scholarships are awarded for general excellence with an emphasis on academic achievement combined with high achievement in at least one of the following: the arts, sport, leadership, science and technology, community service or social justice. Winners must demonstrate a strong connection to the College's cornerstones, particularly international mindedness. The awarding of scholarships is based on an initial application followed by a panel interview for shortlisted students. The scholarship program is supported by the Katsumata Circle, a philanthropic community honouring the College's founders.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7-11 who could not otherwise attend the College for financial reasons. Must have an application for enrolment at the College. Application must include a maximum two-page summary of achievements, two references (one from current school and one attesting to excellence in listed areas), a maximum 500-word statement on why they wish to attend, and a maximum 500-word statement on what international mindedness means to them. Students from indigenous backgrounds are encouraged to apply.
special_conditions: Available from 2027 entry onwards. Students from indigenous backgrounds are encouraged to apply. Must demonstrate connection to College's international mindedness cornerstone.

overview: Dedicated scholarships page offering academic, choral, instrumental, and visual arts scholarships.
Dedicated scholarships page found with detailed information about scholarship types and application process.
Dedicated scholarship page with comprehensive information on Foundation Scholarships, General Excellence, Academic, Music and Sport scholarships.
Dedicated scholarship page with multiple scholarship types including academic, music, and need-based Open Circle Scholarships.
Dedicated scholarships page with comprehensive information on academic, music, piano, and vocal scholarships.
Dedicated scholarship page offering academic, boarding, music and first nations scholarships.
Dedicated scholarships page with multiple scholarship types including academic, general excellence, first nations, and music scholarships.

school_name: Melbourne Montessori College - Brighton East Campus
overview: The MMC Montessorian Scholarship is awarded to students entering Years 7 to 11 and is open to both existing MMC families and prospective families. This scholarship supports students who embody Montessori values and demonstrates financial need, with both full and partial fee remission options available.
description: In the spirit of creating an inclusive and diverse community, MMC gifts scholarships to current and prospective students so they can experience an independent Montessori education. The scholarship recognizes students who would otherwise not have access to an independent Montessori education due to financial constraints, difficult circumstances, and/or who are Indigenous Australian. Both full and partial fee remission options are available depending on individual circumstances. Applicants must demonstrate sound academic ability and embody Montessori values including self-respect, acceptance of uniqueness and dignity of the individual, kindness, peacefulness, compassion, empathy, honour, individual responsibility, and courage to speak from the heart.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7 to 11. Applicants must be currently at Melbourne Montessori College or attending another school/centre, and in primary or secondary years. Must be Australian citizens and permanent Australian residents within the meaning of the Australian Citizenship Act 1948. Must submit the MMC Scholarship Application Form and supporting documentation. Family must show intent to keep the child/ren enrolled at Melbourne Montessori College for the duration of the scholarship. Open to existing MMC families and prospective families.
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Eligible applicants will be shortlisted for interview with the Principal based on written application addressing selection criteria, references and most recent school reports. Family must agree to accept Melbourne Montessori College Terms and Conditions of Enrolment and Terms of Business.

school_name: Melbourne Montessori College - Brighton East Campus
overview: MMC Bursaries are available for students whose families are facing financial hardship and require fee relief to continue attending Melbourne Montessori College. These means-tested bursaries support current students experiencing difficult financial circumstances.
description: MMS Bursaries are available for current students whose families are facing financial hardship and require fee relief to continue attending Melbourne Montessori College. The bursary program reflects the school's commitment to an inclusive and diverse community by providing financial assistance to families in need. All bursaries are means-tested based on the family's financial circumstances. These are designed to help students who are already enrolled at the school continue their education when financial difficulties arise.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current Melbourne Montessori College students whose families are facing financial hardship. Means-tested bursary based on family income and financial circumstances. Student must be enrolled at Melbourne Montessori College.
special_conditions: Means-tested based on financial circumstances. Must be current student at Melbourne Montessori College.

school_name: Melbourne Montessori College - Caulfield South Campus
overview: MMC Bursaries are awarded to current students whose families are facing financial hardship and require fee relief to continue attending Melbourne Montessori College. This program provides direct fee reduction support to help families maintain their children's enrollment.
description: MMC Bursaries are designed to support current MMC students whose families experience financial difficulties and would otherwise be unable to continue their education at the college. The bursaries provide fee relief to help eligible families manage tuition costs and keep their children enrolled. Applications are assessed based on financial need and family circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be current Melbourne Montessori College students whose families are facing financial hardship; families must be Australian citizens or permanent Australian residents within the meaning of the Australian Citizenship Act 1948; must complete the MMC Scholarship Application Form with supporting documentation.
special_conditions: Families must show intent to keep child/children enrolled at MMC for the duration of the scholarship; renewal requires annual confirmation of ongoing financial need.

school_name: Melbourne Montessori College - Caulfield South Campus
overview: The MMC Montessorian Scholarship is awarded to students entering Years 7 to 11 and is open to both existing MMC families and prospective families. Full and partial fee remission options are available, supporting students who demonstrate sound academic ability and Montessori values.
description: The MMC Montessorian Scholarship recognises academic excellence and embodiment of Montessori values including self-respect, acceptance of individual uniqueness and dignity, kindness, peacefulness, compassion, empathy, honour, individual responsibility, and courage. The scholarship offers flexible options with both full and partial fee remission available to support eligible students. Applicants are assessed through application, references, school reports, and interviews with the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be entering Years 7 to 11; open to existing MMC students and prospective students; must demonstrate sound academic ability; must embody Montessori values of self-respect, acceptance of uniqueness and dignity of the individual, kindness, peacefulness, compassion, empathy, honour, individual responsibility, and courage; must be Australian citizens or permanent residents; must complete the MMC Scholarship Application Form.
special_conditions: Full and partial fee options available; families must show intent to keep child/children enrolled at MMC for the duration of the scholarship; must maintain high standards across studies and school engagement.

school_name: Melbourne Montessori College - Caulfield South Campus
overview: The MMC Senior School Scholarship is awarded to current MMC Junior School students (Year 5-6) to support their continued education at the MMC Senior School as they transition to Year 7. This is a full fee scholarship that endures until completion of Year 12.
description: The MMC Senior School Scholarship is specifically designed to support MMC Junior School students who are unable to continue their education at the MMC Senior School due to financial constraints. The scholarship is a full fee remission that covers tuition costs from the year the student enters Year 7 through to completion of Year 12. The scholarship demonstrates MMC's commitment to diversity and inclusion by ensuring capable students can continue their Montessori education regardless of financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Applicants must be existing MMC Junior School students in Year 5 or Year 6 in 2025 (scholarship awarded upon commencement of Year 7); must be Australian citizens or permanent residents at time of application; must demonstrate sound academic ability; must embody Montessori values of respect of self and others, acceptance of uniqueness and dignity of the individual, kindness, peacefulness, compassion, empathy, honour, individual responsibility, independence, and courage to speak from the heart.
special_conditions: Full fee scholarship through to completion of Year 12; families must confirm ongoing financial need annually for scholarship renewal; open only to existing MMC Junior School students; assessment includes interviews and trials.

overview: School has a dedicated scholarship page featuring music, academic, sports, and general excellence scholarships.
Scholarship for students entering Year 7 and Year 10 that recognises high-level academic achievement. Recipients receive a discount on tuition fees until the end of Year 9 and Year 12 respectively.
description: Academic Excellence Scholarships are awarded to students demonstrating a high level of academic achievement, as evidenced by previous academic results, referrals, awards and testing. Recipients receive a reduced tuition component of their school fees for the duration of the scholarship period at a value determined by the scholarship committee. Recipients must maintain an 'Above Standard' academic performance across all core subjects (English, Mathematics, Biblical Studies, Science and Humanities) and display College values through their behaviour.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 or Year 10. Must demonstrate high level of academic achievement through previous academic results, referrals, awards and testing. Must be currently enrolled or have an active application for future enrolment at the college. Must maintain 'Above Standard' academic performance across all core subjects.
special_conditions: Scholarship recipients required to maintain consistently high academic efforts and display College values through behaviour. Recipients required to maintain 'Above Standard' academic performance across all core subjects.

overview: Scholarship for students entering Year 7 and Year 10 recognising significant achievement in co-curricular activities or social justice initiatives. Recipients receive a discount on tuition fees until the end of Year 9 and Year 12 respectively.
description: Co-Curricular Excellence Scholarships are awarded to students demonstrating significant level of achievement in a particular co-curricular or social justice activity. Applicants should excel in a wide range of interests and talents (normally at an elite level) or contribute exceptional value to their school or church community. Recipients receive a reduced tuition component of their school fees for the duration of the scholarship period. Recipients must maintain an 'At Standard or Above' academic performance across all core subjects (English, Mathematics, Biblical Studies, Science and Humanities) and continuously display College values through behaviour.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 or Year 10. Must demonstrate significant achievement in a particular co-curricular or social justice activity, normally at elite level. Must excel in wide range of interests and talents or contribute exceptional value to school or church community. Must be currently enrolled or have an active application for future enrolment. Must maintain 'At Standard or Above' academic performance across all core subjects.
special_conditions: Applicants should demonstrate elite-level achievement or exceptional contribution to school/church community. Recipients must continuously display College values through behaviour. Academic performance maintained at 'At Standard or Above' level.

overview: Scholarship for senior students passionate about leadership seeking to develop their leadership skills and understanding. Provides a discount on tuition fees for the duration of the scholarship period.
description: Leadership Excellence Scholarships are aimed at senior students who are passionate about leadership and are looking for opportunities to grow their skills and understanding of leadership. Recipients receive a reduced tuition component of their school fees for the duration of the scholarship period at a value determined by the scholarship committee. Recipients must demonstrate excellence in leadership qualities and commit to contributing to college life while supporting its Christian ethos.
eligibility_criteria: Senior students (Year 11-12 implied) who are passionate about leadership. Must demonstrate leadership qualities and commitment to college life. Must be currently enrolled or have an active application for future enrolment. Must display College values through behaviour and maintain appropriate academic performance.
special_conditions: Limited to senior students. Must demonstrate passion for and commitment to leadership development. Must contribute to college life and support Christian ethos. May be interviewed as part of selection process.

overview: Dedicated scholarship page with detailed information on academic, music, sports, performing arts, science, boarding, and means-tested scholarships.
Dedicated scholarship and bursaries page with multiple programs including Ruben Fried Scholarship, Gesher Bursaries, and performing arts scholarships.

The Academic Scholarship recognises outstanding academic achievement and is awarded to students who demonstrate excellence through entrance examination. This scholarship is open to both current and prospective students entering Year 7 or Year 10, and provides significant tuition fee remission.
description: The Academic Scholarship is designed to recognise and reward students with exceptional academic ability. Applicants must achieve a high score in the College's scholarship examination and demonstrate academic excellence. The scholarship provides tuition fee remission of up to 70%, with the final percentage determined by the Principal. Students entering Year 7 receive the scholarship for six years, while students entering Year 10 receive it for three years. Multiple scholarships may be applied for, but only one can be held at any time.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective students entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2025, 2026 or 2027. Must be an Australian citizen, permanent resident, or on a funded temporary VISA. Must sit the scholarship examination and attend an interview with the Principal if invited. Open to both domestic and internal applicants.
special_conditions: Scholarship valid for 6 years (Year 7 entry) or 3 years (Year 10 entry). Only one scholarship can be held at any time despite multiple applications.

overview: The General Excellence Scholarship recognises students who display special talents or interests across a broad range of areas including sports, music, art, and the physical environment. This scholarship rewards all-round excellence and is available to both current and prospective students entering Year 7 or Year 10.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is designed for students who demonstrate special talents or interests in diverse areas such as sports, music, visual arts, and environmental studies. This scholarship recognises all-round achievement beyond academics and values students' contributions to the school community. Recipients receive tuition fee remission of up to 70%, with the final percentage at the discretion of the Principal. The scholarship is awarded for six years to Year 7 entrants and three years to Year 10 entrants. Applicants must provide supporting documentation from a school, mentor, community organisation or club.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective students entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2025, 2026 or 2027. Must be an Australian citizen, permanent resident, or on a funded temporary VISA. Must demonstrate special talents or interests in physical environment, sports, music, art or related fields. Must provide at least one written reference from school, mentor, community organisation or club. Must attend an interview with the Principal if invited.
special_conditions: Requires at least one written reference from school, mentor, community organisation or club. Scholarship valid for 6 years (Year 7 entry) or 3 years (Year 10 entry). Only one scholarship can be held at any time.

overview: The Christian Leadership Scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate leadership qualities aligned with the College's Christian values. This scholarship is available to current and prospective students entering Year 10 and recognises students who embody the College's mission and demonstrate strong character.
description: The Christian Leadership Scholarship recognises students who display leadership qualities consistent with Christian values and the College's mission of 'Transforming lives through Christ and the wonder of learning'. Recipients receive tuition fee remission of up to 70%, determined at the Principal's discretion. The scholarship is awarded for three years to Year 10 entrants. Applicants must demonstrate alignment with the College's Christian values and leadership potential. A letter of recommendation from a Church pastor or minister is required, demonstrating the student's Christian faith and leadership in their faith community.
eligibility_criteria: Current or prospective students entering Year 10 in 2025. Must be an Australian citizen, permanent resident, or on a funded temporary VISA. Must demonstrate Christian leadership qualities aligned with College values. Must submit a completed application with all required supporting documents and application fee. Must provide a letter of recommendation from Church pastor or minister. Must attend an interview with the Principal if invited.
special_conditions: Requires letter of recommendation from Church pastor or minister. Scholarship valid for 3 years (Year 10 entry). Only one scholarship can be held at any time.

school_name: Overnewton Anglican Community College - Canowindra Campus
overview: Overnewton offers Academic Scholarships to outstanding students entering Year 5. These scholarships are awarded on the basis of academic merit and continue through to Year 12, providing long-term support for students who demonstrate a strong commitment to learning.
description: The College recognises and nurtures academic potential through its Academic Scholarship Program. Scholarships are offered for entry into Year 5 and continue through to the completion of Year 12. The program is open to both current Overnewton students and those attending other schools. Scholarship holders must maintain academic excellence and demonstrate commitment to the College values of Learning, Excellence, Respect and Community. The College awards a select number of scholarships each year based on merit.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 5. Open to both current Overnewton students and those attending other schools. Applicants must sit the ACER Scholarship Examination. Academic merit is the primary criterion for selection.
special_conditions: Scholarships carry through to Year 12. Any existing bursary or financial support is replaced by the scholarship if it provides more benefit. Must maintain standards to retain scholarship.

school_name: Overnewton Anglican Community College - Canowindra Campus
overview: Overnewton offers Academic Scholarships to outstanding students entering Year 7. These scholarships are awarded on the basis of academic merit and continue through to Year 12, providing long-term support for students who demonstrate a strong commitment to learning.
description: The College recognises and nurtures academic potential through its Academic Scholarship Program. Scholarships are offered for entry into Year 7 and continue through to the completion of Year 12. The program is open to both current Overnewton students and those attending other schools. Scholarship holders must maintain academic excellence and demonstrate commitment to the College values of Learning, Excellence, Respect and Community. The College awards a select number of scholarships each year based on merit.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7. Open to both current Overnewton students and those attending other schools. Applicants must sit the ACER Scholarship Examination (Level 1 test). Academic merit is the primary criterion for selection.
special_conditions: Scholarships carry through to Year 12. Any existing bursary or financial support is replaced by the scholarship if it provides more benefit. Must maintain standards to retain scholarship.

school_name: Overnewton Anglican Community College - Canowindra Campus
