overview: Overnewton offers Academic Scholarships to outstanding students entering Year 9. These scholarships are awarded on the basis of academic merit and continue through to Year 12, providing long-term support for students who demonstrate a strong commitment to learning.
description: The College recognises and nurtures academic potential through its Academic Scholarship Program. Scholarships are offered for entry into Year 9 and continue through to the completion of Year 12. The program is open to both current Overnewton students and those attending other schools. Scholarship holders must maintain academic excellence and demonstrate commitment to the College values of Learning, Excellence, Respect and Community. The College awards a select number of scholarships each year based on merit.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 9. Open to both current Overnewton students and those attending other schools. Applicants must sit the ACER Scholarship Examination (Level 2 test). Academic merit is the primary criterion for selection.
special_conditions: Scholarships carry through to Year 12. Any existing bursary or financial support is replaced by the scholarship if it provides more benefit. Must maintain standards to retain scholarship.

overview: Dedicated scholarships page with multiple scholarship programs including Academic, Means-Tested, and Principal's Discretionary scholarships.
Dedicated scholarships page with comprehensive information about academic, general excellence, and special scholarships.

Penleigh and Essendon Grammar School invites students with an outstanding academic record to apply for Academic Scholarships. These scholarships offer full or part remission of tuition fees and continue to the conclusion of Year 12, conditional upon sound academic progress and active participation in the school's co-curricular programme.
description: Academic Scholarships recognize students with exceptional academic achievement. These scholarships offer full or part remission of tuition fees, though they do not apply to school development or the composite fee component of annual fees. Scholarships are available for new and continuing students entering Year 7, with new students also eligible for entry into Year 9 and Year 11. For current PEGS students entering Year 9 or Year 11, academic progress and examination results are used to assess eligibility. Recipients must maintain sound academic progress and active participation in the school's co-curricular programme, with progress reviewed annually.
eligibility_criteria: Students with an outstanding academic record entering Years 7, 9, or 11. Students must be living permanently in Australia. Students currently holding ongoing scholarships in another school are not eligible. Overseas full fee paying students are not eligible. Shortlisted applicants must bring copies of their most recent school reports and NAPLAN test results to their interview.
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or Partial Fee Remission
special_conditions: Must demonstrate sound academic progress. Must participate actively in school's co-curricular programme. Progress reviewed annually.

overview: Penleigh and Essendon Grammar School invites students with outstanding musical ability to apply for Excellence in Music Scholarships. Scholarship recipients must commit to the school and its music programme, with progress reviewed annually.
description: Excellence in Music Scholarships recognize students with outstanding musical ability. These scholarships provide remission of tuition fees at the level specified at interview and in the formal offer. Scholarships are available to both prospective students wishing to enter the school and current students already attending. Scholarships are tenable to the end of Year 12. Recipients must commit to the school and its music programme, with their progress reviewed annually.
eligibility_criteria: Students with outstanding musical ability entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10, or 11. Students must apply in the year prior to entry (i.e., Year 6, 7, 8, 9, or 10). Students living permanently in Australia are eligible. Students currently holding ongoing scholarships at another school will not be considered. Overseas full fee paying students are not eligible. Applicants must submit copies of their most recent school reports and NAPLAN test results with their application.
special_conditions: Must commit to school music programme. Progress reviewed annually. Auditions held on Saturday 21 February 2026 (for 2027 entry).

overview: Penleigh and Essendon Grammar School's General Excellence Awards recognize sound academic achievement and outstanding ability in performing arts (music, drama, dance), sports, leadership, and contribution to school life and the wider community. Award recipients must commit to the school and its co-curricular programmes.
description: General Excellence Awards recognize students who demonstrate sound academic achievement and outstanding ability in one or more of the following areas: contribution to the performing arts (music, drama, and dance), sports participation and achievement, leadership, and contribution to school life and the wider community. A sound academic standard is required. Award recipients must commit to the school and its co-curricular programmes, with their progress reviewed annually. These awards acknowledge students who excel across multiple dimensions of school life.
eligibility_criteria: Students with significant experience and achievement in performing arts, sports, leadership, and/or community contribution. Sound academic achievement required. Awards are available to both current and prospective students. Students must be living permanently in Australia. Students currently holding ongoing scholarships at another school are not eligible. Overseas full fee paying students are not eligible.
special_conditions: Must demonstrate achievement in multiple areas. Sound academic standard required. Progress reviewed annually.

school_name: Penleigh & Essendon Grammar School - Essendon Campus
overview: Penleigh and Essendon Grammar School invites students with outstanding academic records to apply for Academic Scholarships. These scholarships offer full or part remission of tuition fees for eligible students entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11.
description: Academic Scholarships provide full or partial remission of tuition fees to recognise outstanding academic achievement. The scholarships do not apply to school development or the composite fee component of annual fees. Students currently holding ongoing scholarships at another school will not be considered, and awards are not available to overseas full fee paying students. Shortlisted applicants are required to provide copies of their most recent school reports and NAPLAN test results.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding academic record required. Students entering Year 9 or Year 11 use semester and examination results for assessment. Most recent school reports and NAPLAN test results required for shortlisted applicants. Students holding ongoing scholarships at another school ineligible. Overseas full fee paying students ineligible.
special_conditions: Must be assessed through examination for Year 7 entry. Year 9 and Year 11 assessed by semester and examination results. Scholarship values not publicly disclosed.

school_name: Penleigh & Essendon Grammar School - Essendon Campus
overview: Penleigh and Essendon Grammar School offers Excellence in Music Scholarships for students with outstanding musical ability. These scholarships are available to both prospective and current students entering Year 7 through Year 11.
description: Excellence in Music Scholarships recognise outstanding musical ability and commitment to music education. Scholarship recipients must commit to the school and its music programme, with progress reviewed annually. The scholarships provide remission of tuition fees at levels specified at interview and in the formal offer letter. Scholarships are tenable to the end of Year 12. Students must apply in the year prior to their year of entry (e.g., Year 6 students apply for Year 7 entry).
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding musical ability required. Students must apply in the year prior to entry (Year 6, 7, 8, 9, or 10 apply for entry to Year 7, 8, 9, 10, or 11 respectively). Students holding ongoing scholarships at another school ineligible. Overseas full fee paying students ineligible. School reports and NAPLAN test results required with application.
special_conditions: Audition required. Recipients must commit to school music programme. Progress reviewed annually. Scholarships not disclosed publicly.

school_name: Penleigh & Essendon Grammar School - Essendon Campus

Award recipients are required to commit to the school and its co-curricular programmes. Students with significant experience and achievement in these fields are invited to apply.
eligibility_criteria: Sound academic standard required. Significant experience and achievement in some or all of the following: performing arts (music, drama, dance), leadership, and community contribution. Awards require demonstrated commitment to co-curricular programmes.
special_conditions: Recipients must commit to school and co-curricular programmes. Value not publicly disclosed.

school_name: Penleigh & Essendon Grammar School - Moonee Ponds Campus
overview: Penleigh and Essendon Grammar School invites students with outstanding academic records to apply for Academic Scholarships. These scholarships offer full or part remission of tuition fees and continue to Year 12, conditional upon sound academic progress and active participation in co-curricular programs.
description: Academic Scholarships are offered to students entering Year 7, Year 9, and Year 11. Students in Years 7 must sit a competitive scholarship examination, while students entering Year 9 or Year 11 are assessed using their most recent semester and examination results. The scholarships provide full or part remission of tuition fees, though they do not apply to school development or the composite fee component of annual fees. Academic Scholarships are conditional upon continued sound academic progress and active participation in the school's co-curricular programs. Students currently holding ongoing scholarships at another school are not eligible, nor are overseas full fee paying students.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding academic record required. Must be Australian resident. Students entering Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11 are eligible. Students currently holding ongoing scholarships at another school are not eligible. Overseas full fee paying students are not eligible. Applicants must provide most recent school reports and NAPLAN test results.
special_conditions: Scholarship continues to Year 12 conditional upon criteria including sound academic progress and active participation in school co-curricular programs. Does not apply to school development or composite fee component. Must be Australian resident.

school_name: Penleigh & Essendon Grammar School - Moonee Ponds Campus
overview: Penleigh and Essendon Grammar School invites students with outstanding musical ability to apply for Excellence in Music Scholarships. These scholarships recognize exceptional musical talent and require recipients to commit to the school and its music program.
description: Excellence in Music Scholarships are available to prospective students wishing to enter the school as well as current students. Recipients must commit to the school and its music program, with progress reviewed annually. Scholarship terms and conditions apply. Applicants must provide most recent school reports and NAPLAN test results with their application. Students currently holding ongoing scholarships at another school are not eligible, nor are overseas full fee paying students.
eligibility_criteria: Outstanding musical ability required. Available to students entering Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, or Year 11. Students must apply in the year prior to entry (Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, or Year 10). Must be Australian resident. Students currently holding ongoing scholarships at another school are not eligible. Overseas full fee paying students are not eligible.
special_conditions: Recipients required to commit to school and music program. Progress reviewed annually. Must apply in year prior to entry.

school_name: Penleigh & Essendon Grammar School - Moonee Ponds Campus
overview: The General Excellence Awards recognize students with sound academic achievement and outstanding ability in performing arts, leadership, and contribution to school and community. These awards are designed to foster development of students with special talents.
description: General Excellence Awards recognize sound academic achievement and outstanding ability in some or all of the following areas: contribution to performing arts (including music, drama and dance), leadership and contribution to school life and the wider community. Both current students and prospective students may apply. Award recipients are required to commit to the school and its co-curricular programs, with progress reviewed annually. A sound academic standard is necessary for eligibility.
eligibility_criteria: Sound academic achievement required. Outstanding ability in performing arts (music, drama, dance) and/or leadership and community contribution. Must have significant experience and achievement in at least one of the specified areas. Only students entering Years 7 to 11 are eligible. Both current and prospective students may apply.
special_conditions: Recipients required to commit to school and co-curricular programs. Progress reviewed annually. Sound academic standard is necessary.

Merit-based scholarship for Year 10 and 11 entry recognising student excellence and widening access to Sophia Mundi's exceptional education and International Baccalaureate program. Scholarships range from 50% to 100% of tuition fees and can be awarded for 1-2 years depending on year of entry.
description: The Merit Scholarship celebrates aspiration and achievement at Sophia Mundi Steiner School. These scholarships seek to recognise the capabilities of talented students and are specifically aimed at widening access to the exceptional education provided by Sophia Mundi Steiner School and the International Baccalaureate Diploma Program. Scholarships can be awarded for 1 to 2 years depending on the year of entry and range from 50% to 100% of tuition fees. Awards are based on student merit as measured by an aptitude test, interview, community and cultural involvement, and past school reports indicating academic and behavioural standards.
eligibility_criteria: Open to future students entering Year 10 or 11. Assessment based on aptitude test, interview, community and cultural involvement, and past school reports indicating academic and behavioural standards.
special_conditions: Scholarship application form required. Registration fee of $275 required. Scholarship test conducted in Term 4. 2026 scholarships now open, 2025 applications closed.

overview: Music scholarship for Year 10 and 11 entry, both current and new students, recognising excellence in music performance. Awardees must demonstrate minimum AMEB Grade 6 instrumental/vocal performance and Grade 4 music theory at time of application.
description: Music Scholarships may be awarded to current and new students entering Year 10 or 11 who demonstrate excellence in music knowledge and musical performance. Applicants must meet minimum AMEB level requirements of Grade 6 AMEB for instrumental/vocal performance and Grade 4 music theory at time of application. In addition to excellence in music, these scholarships are awarded based on student merit as measured by an aptitude test, interview, community and cultural involvement, and past school reports indicating academic and behavioural standards. Shortlisted students are invited to audition at Sophia Mundi where they perform two contrasting pieces and complete sight reading and aural tests.
eligibility_criteria: Open to current and new students entering Year 10 or 11. Must demonstrate minimum AMEB Level Grade 6 for instrumental/vocal performance and Grade 4 music theory at time of application. Assessment includes aptitude test, interview, community and cultural involvement, and school reports.
special_conditions: Shortlisted students audition at school performing two contrasting pieces. Sight reading and aural tests required. Technical work may be requested. Non-pianists must perform at least one piece with piano accompaniment. Families must arrange external piano accompanists if required. Limited scholarship spaces available. Scholarship application form required. Registration fee of $275 required.

overview: Academic Scholarships are awarded to academically gifted students who are ambitious in their learning and leadership. The scholarship nurtures students' potential and drive for success through their secondary schooling and beyond the school gates as Alumni.
description: Southern Cross Grammar's Academic Scholarships are designed for students who demonstrate exceptional academic ability and leadership potential. The scholarship recognises and supports ambitious learners throughout their secondary education, nurturing their potential and encouraging their drive for success. Recipients benefit from the school's commitment to developing future leaders in the school community and beyond.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7-11 in 2027 who are prospective, new, or currently enrolled at Southern Cross Grammar; Australian Citizen or Permanent Resident at time of application; must sit Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination.
special_conditions: Shortlisted students will be invited for interview with Principal. Prospective families pay $206 total ($100 enrolment fee + $106 exam fee). Current families pay $106 exam fee only.

overview: General Excellence Scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate excellence in their academic pursuits and leadership in school life and beyond in the wider community. As future leaders, the scholarship nurtures their potential, work ethic, and drive to achieve great things.
description: These scholarships recognise students who excel not only academically but also in their broader contributions to school life and community. The program is designed to identify and support leaders who demonstrate strong work ethic and the drive to achieve excellence. Recipients are nurtured through their secondary education to develop into future leaders who make positive contributions beyond the school community.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7-11 in 2027 who are prospective, new, or currently enrolled at Southern Cross Grammar; Australian Citizen or Permanent Resident at time of application; must sit Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination.
special_conditions: Shortlisted students will be invited for interview with Principal. Prospective families pay $206 total ($100 enrolment fee + $106 exam fee). Current families pay $106 exam fee only.

overview: At Southern Cross Grammar, we encourage students' passion for, and excellence in Music. Music Scholarships are designed to support talented musicians entering the school.
description: Southern Cross Grammar offers Music Scholarships to recognise and support students who demonstrate excellence in music. The school is committed to encouraging students' passion for and excellence in this discipline. These scholarships provide financial support to talented musicians who wish to pursue their musical interests at secondary level within the school's environment.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7-11 in 2027 who are prospective, new, or currently enrolled at Southern Cross Grammar; Australian Citizen or Permanent Resident at time of application; applicants must participate in Music Scholarship Auditions (not required to sit Edutest exam).
special_conditions: Music Auditions commence in week of 23 February 2026. Students are not required to sit Edutest Academic Scholarship Examination. Shortlisted students invited for interview with Principal.

overview: Indigenous Student Scholarships provide opportunities to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students to attend Southern Cross Grammar at a significant discount. The scholarships are part of the School's commitment to help improve education outcomes for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students at the Secondary schooling level.
description: Southern Cross Grammar offers Indigenous Scholarships specifically designed to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. These scholarships provide substantial financial assistance, allowing Indigenous students to access quality secondary education. The program reflects the school's commitment to improving educational outcomes and creating pathways for Indigenous students in secondary education.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students entering Years 7-11 in 2027; prospective, new, or currently enrolled at Southern Cross Grammar; Australian Citizen or Permanent Resident at time of application.
special_conditions: Described as providing 'significant discount' on fees. Specific assessment requirements not detailed on main scholarships page.

The scholarship provides financial support that can significantly offset tuition fees.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Warragul Campus
overview: Offered to students who demonstrate their potential to be outstanding contributors in their chosen fields including sport, performing arts, visual arts, STEM, business/entrepreneurship, community service/leadership and environmental awareness.

Recipients are hard-working students of excellent character who are keen to make valuable contributions to the school community. This scholarship can significantly offset tuition fees.

Must be hard-working students of excellent character willing to make valuable contributions to school community.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Warragul Campus
overview: Offered under the School's Education Support Program to new students for entry in Years 8 to 11 in 2026 at the Traralgon Campus. Provides places to students continuing through to Year 12.
description: The Foundation Scholarship is offered under St Paul's Education Support Program to dedicated and capable students who would benefit from attending St Paul's but may be unable to do so without financial assistance. Students will have a genuine desire to achieve their potential, be committed to their studies, be positive influences on those around them and have desire to participate in an environment where learning is valued and respected. These places are available at the Traralgon Campus for students continuing through to Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students for entry in Years 8 to 11 in 2026 at Traralgon Campus only. Must be dedicated and capable students who would benefit from attending St Paul's but for financial or other circumstances may be unable to do so. Must have genuine desire to achieve potential, commitment to studies, positive influence on others, and desire to participate in learning-focused environment.
special_conditions: Only available at Traralgon Campus for entry in 2026. Students must be committed to continuing through to Year 12.

school_name: The Hamilton and Alexandra College - Junior Campus
overview: The Ivy Scholarship recognises meritorious achievement, a passion for continuous learning and strong character strengths. It is available to well-rounded and curious students entering Years 4, 5, 6, and 9-12 who display an interest in the world around them.
description: Ivy Scholarships are awarded to students demonstrating meritorious achievement and strong character strengths with a passion for continuous learning. These scholarships are available for general excellence applications in Years 4-6 and boarding scholarships in Years 9-12. Applicants are assessed based on ability in academic and co-curricular areas, school reports, and interview results. Boarding scholarships are available for students who wish to join the boarding house community, with requirements matching the general excellence application process.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 4, 5, 6, or Years 9-12. Must demonstrate meritorious achievement, character strengths, and a passion for continuous learning. Well-rounded students with an interest in the world around them.
year_levels: Year 4, Year 5, Year 6, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12 or Multiple
special_conditions: Applicants assessed based on academic ability, co-curricular achievement, school reports and interviews.

school_name: The Hamilton and Alexandra College - Junior Campus
overview: The Tower Scholarship is offered by The Hamilton and Alexandra College Foundation to support students who aspire and prioritise a quality, independent education. These scholarships are available for boarding students in Years 9-12.

Available for boarding students in Years 9-12, these scholarships encourage applications from families who value educational excellence. The Foundation actively fundraises to provide these scholarships as part of its commitment to offering opportunities for worthy students.
eligibility_criteria: Students in Years 9-12 seeking boarding scholarships. Applicants must prioritise and aspire to receive a quality, independent education.
year_levels: Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12 or Multiple
special_conditions: Available for boarding students only.

school_name: The Hamilton and Alexandra College - Junior Campus
overview: This bursary provides 100% reduction of boarding fees for one year to Year 7 and 8 students who live more than 100km from Hamilton. It is available to both new and current College families and designed to support regional and rural families.
description: The Year 7 & 8 Boarding Bursary is a 100% reduction of boarding fees for one year, designed to support families living more than 100km from Hamilton, Victoria 3300. The bursary does not cover textbooks, uniforms, excursions, technology fees, individual subject fees or tuition fees. All students receiving this bursary are required to remain boarding at the College through to the end of Year 12, or must repay the bursary in full. Applications are open year-round.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 or Year 8 students. Must live more than 100km from Hamilton, Victoria 3300. Open to both new and current College families.
special_conditions: Students must remain boarding through to end of Year 12 or repay bursary in full. Does not cover textbooks, uniforms, excursions, technology fees, individual subject fees or tuition fees.

school_name: The Hamilton and Alexandra College - Junior Campus
overview: Equity Scholarships are designed to support families who require financial assistance and who understand and prioritise the benefit of receiving a quality, independent education. The College is passionate about supporting worthy students who cannot afford full tuition fees.
description: Equity Scholarships are designed to support families requiring financial assistance to access quality independent education at the College. The College is committed to supporting worthy students who cannot afford full tuition fees so they may benefit from the extraordinary education offered. These scholarships recognise that financial barriers should not prevent access to educational excellence. Applications are open year-round.
eligibility_criteria: Families demonstrating financial need. Must prioritise and value quality independent education. Students who are otherwise unable to afford full tuition fees.
special_conditions: Assessment based on demonstrated financial need and commitment to education.

overview: Dedicated scholarship page with comprehensive information on multiple scholarship types including academic, equity, music, performing arts, and STEM scholarships.

The Knox School offers Academic Scholarships for exceptional students demonstrating outstanding academic achievement and potential. Available for Years 7, 9, and 10 entry in 2027, these scholarships are open to external, current and future TKS students. The amount offered is at the discretion of the Principal and represents a binding commitment until completion of Year 12.
description: Academic Scholarships recognise demonstrated academic achievement, excellence in one or more academic areas (STEM, Arts, Humanities etc), high level of engagement with all curriculum and co-curricular activities, evidence of leadership, and positive contribution to school activities. Applicants must complete an entrance examination through EduTest and submit a portfolio including school reports, NAPLAN results, and supporting documents. Selection is based on examination results and portfolio assessment, with shortlisted applicants invited to meet the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7, 9, or 10 in 2027. Open to external, current, and future TKS students. Must be Australian citizens or permanent residents (students on temporary or student visas are not eligible). Must demonstrate academic achievement and potential, commitment to Knox School Values, and engagement with curriculum and co-curricular activities.
special_conditions: Scholarship commitment is binding until completion of Year 12. Applicants must submit portfolio including school reports and NAPLAN results. Exam results remain property of The Knox School and are not provided to parents.

overview: The Knox School offers General Excellence Scholarships recognising students who demonstrate excellence across multiple pathways. Available in Sport, Entrepreneurship, and Service categories, these scholarships acknowledge all-round achievement beyond academics.
description: General Excellence Scholarships are awarded to students demonstrating excellence in specific pathways: Sport (outstanding athletic achievement and potential), Entrepreneurship (innovative thinking and business acumen), or Service (significant community contribution and leadership). These scholarships recognise students who contribute meaningfully to the school community through their chosen pathway while maintaining good academic standing. The amount offered is at the discretion of the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Students demonstrating excellence in Sport, Entrepreneurship, or Service pathways. Must be Australian citizens or permanent residents. Must show commitment to Knox School Values and make a positive contribution to the school community.
special_conditions: Scholarship commitment is binding until completion of Year 12. Applicants selected for specific pathways: Sport, Entrepreneurship, or Service.

overview: The Knox School (referred to as TKS Falconians) offers Alumni Scholarships to children of former students. This scholarship programme recognises the school's connection with past students and their families, encouraging generational involvement in the school community.
description: Alumni Scholarships provide opportunities for children and relatives of Knox School alumni (Falconians) to access scholarship support. These scholarships strengthen the connection between the school and its alumni community. The amount offered is at the discretion of the Principal and represents a binding commitment until completion of Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be a direct descendant or relative of a Knox School alumnus (Falconian). Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident. Applicant must demonstrate reasonable academic standing and commitment to school values.
special_conditions: Applicant must be a direct relative of a Knox School alumnus. Scholarship commitment is binding until completion of Year 12.

overview: The Knox School offers Young Entrepreneurs Scholarships recognising students with innovative thinking, business acumen, and entrepreneurial spirit. This scholarship is available as part of the General Excellence pathway, specifically targeting students demonstrating entrepreneurial potential.
description: Young Entrepreneurs Scholarships acknowledge students who demonstrate innovative thinking, entrepreneurial spirit, and potential to create positive change through business or social enterprise initiatives. These scholarships are awarded to students who show evidence of entrepreneurial thinking, leadership in student business ventures, or innovative project development. Recipients are expected to contribute to the school community through their entrepreneurial activities and maintain good academic standing.
eligibility_criteria: Students demonstrating entrepreneurial spirit, innovative thinking, and business acumen. Must be Australian citizens or permanent residents. Must show commitment to Knox School Values and evidence of entrepreneurial activity or potential.
special_conditions: Scholarship commitment is binding until completion of Year 12. Applicants should demonstrate evidence of entrepreneurial activity, innovative projects, or business ventures.

Trinity Lutheran College offers an Academic Scholarship worth 50% of tuition fees for 12 months. One scholarship is awarded to a primary school student and one to a secondary school student entering in 2026. This scholarship recognises students demonstrating high levels of academic achievement.

Successful applicants receive 50% tuition fee remission for 12 months. The scholarship application and selection process takes place in Term 2-3 each year for the following year. overview: This scholarship group is open specifically to baptised members of Holy Trinity Lutheran Church, Mildura. It provides support for eligible church members' children attending Trinity Lutheran College.

overview: The Australian International Academy of Education offers Academic Scholarships to recognise and support academically gifted students. These scholarships are available in Full, Half, and Quarter amounts across multiple campuses, with a total of 35 scholarships available annually. The scholarships are designed to ensure that academically talented students can access the school's world-class academic programmes including IB and VCE options regardless of financial constraints.
