overview: The Australian International Academy of Education offers Quran Memorisation & Tajweed Scholarships to recognise students who excel in Islamic studies, specifically in memorising the Quran and mastering Tajweed (proper recitation). This scholarship reflects the school's commitment to Islamic values and spiritual excellence.
description: Quran Memorisation & Tajweed Scholarships are merit-based awards specifically designed to support and recognise students who demonstrate exceptional ability in memorising the Quran and mastering Tajweed. These scholarships are part of AIAE's integrated approach to Islamic education, which emphasises the development of students' spiritual and Islamic knowledge alongside their academic studies. The scholarships acknowledge the dedication and discipline required to memorise the Quran and perfect its recitation according to Islamic principles.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students who demonstrate exceptional ability in Quran memorisation and Tajweed (proper recitation). Eligible applicants must show commitment to Islamic studies and excellence in this specialised area. Assessment based on Quranic knowledge, memorisation level, and recitation proficiency.
special_conditions: Specifically for students excelling in Quran memorisation and Tajweed

school_name: Australian International Academy of Education - Caroline Springs Primary Campus
overview: Academic scholarships are offered across all three AIA campuses including Caroline Springs Primary Campus. The school offers Full, Half, and Quarter scholarship options for both local and overseas students.
description: The Australian International Academy offers Academic Scholarships as part of a total of 35 Full, Half, and Quarter Scholarships available across all Melbourne campuses. These scholarships recognize academic excellence and are available to primary and secondary students. Academic Scholarship exams are run by EduTest, an organization that conducts testing for approximately 100 schools across Australia. Practice exams and detailed exam structure information are available to prospective applicants.
eligibility_criteria: Open to both local and overseas students. Available for Foundation through Year 12 students across all AIA campuses. No specific academic requirements detailed, though competitive selection process applies.
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction or 25% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarships are available for Full, Half, and Quarter amounts. Testing conducted by EduTest in August 2025.

school_name: Australian International Academy of Education - Caroline Springs Primary Campus
overview: The Qur'aan Kareem Scholarship recognizes excellence in Qur'an memorization and Tajweed (proper recitation). It is part of the school's commitment to Islamic education and is available across all three AIA campuses including Caroline Springs.
description: The Australian International Academy offers Qur'aan Kareem Scholarships specifically for students who excel in Qur'an memorization and Tajweed. The scholarship has different memorization requirements based on year level: Foundation to Year 2 students must memorize one part (Juzz), Years 3-5 students must memorize two parts (Ajza), and Years 6-8 students must memorize three parts (Ajza). The scholarship is part of the school's comprehensive Islamic education program and is available to both local and overseas students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to both local and overseas students. Requirements vary by year level: Foundation-Year 2 (1 Juzz memorized), Year 3-5 (2 Ajza memorized), Years 6-8 (3 Ajza memorized). Available across all AIA campuses.
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction or 25% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Memorization requirements differ by year level. Scholarship available in Full, Half, and Quarter amounts.

school_name: Australian International Academy of Education - Caroline Springs Primary Campus
overview: The Sports Scholarship recognizes and supports students who demonstrate excellence in sports. It is one of six scholarship categories available across AIA campuses including Caroline Springs.
description: The Australian International Academy offers Sports Scholarships to students who excel in athletic pursuits. This scholarship is part of the school's broader program to support all-round student development and is available to both local and overseas students. The school encourages registrations for Sports Scholarship assessment and provides support for prospective applicants.
eligibility_criteria: Open to both local and overseas students. Must demonstrate sporting excellence. Available across all AIA campuses for primary and secondary students.
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction or 25% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarship available in Full, Half, and Quarter amounts. Selection based on sporting ability and achievement.

school_name: Australian International Academy of Education - Caroline Springs Primary Campus
overview: The Arts Scholarship recognizes students who demonstrate excellence in visual and creative arts. It is one of the six scholarship categories offered across AIA campuses including Caroline Springs.
description: The Australian International Academy offers Arts Scholarships to support students with creative talent and artistic excellence. This scholarship is available to both local and overseas students across all AIA campuses. The school provides detailed information regarding Arts Scholarship requirements and assessment criteria to prospective applicants.
eligibility_criteria: Open to both local and overseas students. Must demonstrate excellence in visual or creative arts. Available across all AIA campuses for primary and secondary students.
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction or 25% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Scholarship available in Full, Half, and Quarter amounts. Selection based on artistic ability and creative achievement.

school_name: Australian International Academy of Education - Caroline Springs Senior Campus
overview: The Australian International Academy of Education offers Academic Scholarships to support academically talented students. These scholarships recognize and reward academic excellence.
description: The Australian International Academy of Education offers Academic Scholarships as part of their financial assistance programs. The specific details regarding value, year levels, eligibility criteria, and application processes are available through the school's enrollment office. Prospective families should contact the Caroline Springs Senior Campus directly for comprehensive scholarship information and application requirements.
eligibility_criteria: Information not publicly available - contact school directly at admissions.cs@aia.vic.edu.au or 03 8372 5446

school_name: Australian International Academy of Education - Caroline Springs Senior Campus
overview: The Australian International Academy of Education offers Quran Memorisation & Tajweed Scholarships to support students excelling in Islamic religious studies. These scholarships recognize achievement in Quranic memorization and proper recitation techniques.
description: The Australian International Academy of Education offers Quran Memorisation & Tajweed Scholarships as part of their financial assistance programs. These scholarships support students who demonstrate excellence in memorizing the Quran and mastering proper Quranic recitation (Tajweed). The specific details regarding value, year levels, eligibility criteria, and application processes are available through the school's enrollment office. Prospective families should contact the Caroline Springs Senior Campus directly for comprehensive scholarship information.
eligibility_criteria: Information not publicly available - contact school directly at admissions.cs@aia.vic.edu.au or 03 8372 5446

overview: Full academic scholarships offered to exceptional students demonstrating academic excellence and potential to contribute to the school community. The school seeks students with skills vital for success in the modern world who can contribute meaningfully to the holistic education offered at Bacchus Marsh Grammar.
description: Three Full (100%) Academic Scholarships available for 6 years. These scholarships are open to both current BMG students and external students entering Year 7. The scholarships support high-achieving students in a holistic educational environment that recognizes diverse talents beyond examination results alone.
eligibility_criteria: Current BMG Grade 6 students and external Year 6 students applying for Year 7 entry. Academic merit demonstrated through testing and assessment.

overview: Full tuition scholarship offered to exceptional students demonstrating outstanding academic achievement and potential to contribute meaningfully to the Bacchus Marsh Grammar community. Available through a generous benefactor contribution to the school.
description: Full (100%) scholarship for 6 years offered through the generosity of the van den Hende family. Supports students with demonstrated academic excellence who wish to contribute to the life of the school and embody the holistic educational values of Bacchus Marsh Grammar.
eligibility_criteria: Current BMG Year 8 students and external Year 8 students. Selection based on academic merit and demonstrated potential.
special_conditions: One awarded to current BMG Year 8 student, one to external Year 8 student

sub_type: General Academic Merit with Character Focus
overview: Ballarat Christian College offers scholarships for entry at Year 7, designed to encourage and support new and current students who demonstrate academic excellence and outstanding character attributes. Scholarships are awarded for three years, with the option to reapply in Year 9 for a further three-year scholarship.
description: These scholarships are designed to support students who demonstrate academic excellence and outstanding character attributes. The scholarship provides a reduction to regular applicable tuition fees for three years and does not include the cost of uniforms, books and stationery, extra-curricular activities or other fees and levies. Students may reapply in Year 9 for a further 3-year scholarship to cover the remainder of their secondary schooling at the College. The Principal convenes a scholarship panel to consider all applications, and applicants may be requested to attend an interview at the Principal's discretion.
eligibility_criteria: New and continuing students entering Year 7 who demonstrate academic excellence and outstanding character attributes. Applicants must submit a letter of recommendation from a person who knows the applicant well and can comment on their character, integrity and relevant factors (e.g., church pastor, youth pastor, sports coach, community member). Current enrolments: College will access and take into consideration all Year 5 and Year 6 academic reports to date.
special_conditions: Scholarship covers three years only (Years 7-9). Tuition fees discounted must be reimbursed if the student leaves before the expiration of the scholarship period. Does not include uniforms, books, stationery, extra-curricular activities or other fees and levies.

sub_type: General Academic Merit with Character Focus
overview: Ballarat Christian College offers scholarships for entry at Year 10, designed to encourage and support new and current students who demonstrate academic excellence and outstanding character attributes. Scholarships are awarded for three years covering Years 10-12.
description: These scholarships are designed to support students who demonstrate academic excellence and outstanding character attributes at the Year 10 entry point. The scholarship provides a reduction to regular applicable tuition fees for three years and does not include the cost of uniforms, books and stationery, extra-curricular activities or other fees and levies. The Principal convenes a scholarship panel to consider all applications, and applicants may be requested to attend an interview at the Principal's discretion.
eligibility_criteria: New and continuing students entering Year 10 who demonstrate academic excellence and outstanding character attributes. Applicants must submit a letter of recommendation from a person who knows the applicant well and can comment on their character, integrity and relevant factors (e.g., church pastor, youth pastor, sports coach, community member).
special_conditions: Scholarship covers three years only (Years 10-12). Tuition fees discounted must be reimbursed if the student leaves before the expiration of the scholarship period. Does not include uniforms, books, stationery, extra-curricular activities or other fees and levies.

overview: Ballarat Christian College may be able to provide financial assistance to support families in their choice of Christian Education. The College takes its Christian duty towards families in times of financial hardship very seriously.
description: For families in need of assistance, a degree of fee concession may be available. The College aims to serve its families by charging reasonable fees to support quality outcomes for students and takes very seriously its Christian duty towards families experiencing financial hardship. Families should contact the Business Manager to discuss specific arrangements and options available to them.
eligibility_criteria: Families in need of financial assistance or experiencing financial hardship. Contact the Business Manager to discuss individual circumstances and available concessions.
special_conditions: Individual assessment based on family circumstances. May include fee concessions, sibling discounts, or staff/clergy discounts depending on eligibility.

overview: Bayside Christian College offers scholarships for The Arts to support talented students in co-curricular arts programs. The college recognises achievement in instrumental, band, choir, visual arts and dance.
description: The college offers scholarships for The Arts and encourages students to participate in a variety of co-curricular arts programs including instrumental music, band, choir, visual arts and dance. Scholarships are awarded based on the strength of the candidate's scholarship application, previous school reports, the scholarship test and interview.
eligibility_criteria: Students demonstrating talent and commitment to arts disciplines. Specific academic requirements not publicly detailed.

overview: Bayside Christian College offers a dedicated scholarship for students in their Marching Drumline program. This recognises excellence in percussion and ensemble performance.
description: The college offers scholarships specifically for Marching Drumline participants. Scholarships are awarded based on the strength of the candidate's scholarship application, previous school reports, the scholarship test and interview.
eligibility_criteria: Students with demonstrated ability in drumline/percussion performance. Specific requirements not publicly detailed.

overview: Boronia K-12 College offers Year 7 Scholarships to support students entering the college. The program recognizes student achievement and merit across various dimensions. Limited details available on the official scholarship page.
description: Boronia K-12 College offers Year 7 Scholarships for students entering Year 7 at the college. The scholarship program is designed to support student development and achievement. Further details about the specific value, number of awards, and comprehensive eligibility criteria require direct contact with the school.
eligibility_criteria: For Year 7 entry. Specific academic, artistic, sporting or other merit criteria not publicly detailed on the website.

overview: Braemar College offers an Academic Scholarship for entry at Year 7 to current and potential students. This scholarship recognises academic excellence and is awarded based on examination results conducted by Academic Assessment Services (AAS).
description: The Year 7 Academic Scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating strong academic performance. Awards are generally based on results of examinations held in the previous year. The Principal retains discretion regarding all scholarship and enrolment matters. This is part of Braemar College's commitment to fostering academic excellence among its student cohort.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 7. Applicants must sit examinations conducted by Academic Assessment Services (AAS). No specific gender, residency, or other restrictions stated.
special_conditions: Principal has discretion regarding all enrolment and scholarship matters.

overview: Braemar College offers an Academic Scholarship for entry at Year 10 to current and potential students. This scholarship recognises academic excellence and is awarded based on examination results conducted by Academic Assessment Services (AAS).
description: The Year 10 Academic Scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating strong academic performance. Awards are generally based on results of examinations held in the previous year. The Principal retains discretion regarding all scholarship and enrolment matters. This is part of Braemar College's commitment to fostering academic excellence among its student cohort.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 10. Applicants must sit examinations conducted by Academic Assessment Services (AAS). No specific gender, residency, or other restrictions stated.
special_conditions: Principal has discretion regarding all enrolment and scholarship matters.

overview: Braemar College offers Academic Scholarships for students seeking entry at Year 7. The scholarship is awarded based on examination results conducted by Academic Assessment Services (AAS), with discretion retained by the Principal regarding all enrolment matters.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students demonstrating strong academic merit seeking entry at Year 7. The scholarship is generally awarded based on the results of examinations held in the previous year. Examinations are conducted by Academic Assessment Services (AAS). The Principal has discretion regarding all enrolment matters and scholarship awards.
eligibility_criteria: Current and potential students seeking entry at Year 7. Students must perform well in the AAS examinations held in the previous year.
special_conditions: Examination results from previous year are generally the basis for award. Principal discretion applies.

overview: Braemar College offers Academic Scholarships for students seeking entry at Year 10. The scholarship is awarded based on examination results conducted by Academic Assessment Services (AAS), with discretion retained by the Principal regarding all enrolment matters.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students demonstrating strong academic merit seeking entry at Year 10. The scholarship is generally awarded based on the results of examinations held in the previous year. Examinations are conducted by Academic Assessment Services (AAS). The Principal has discretion regarding all enrolment matters and scholarship awards.
eligibility_criteria: Current and potential students seeking entry at Year 10. Students must perform well in the AAS examinations held in the previous year.
special_conditions: Examination results from previous year are generally the basis for award. Principal discretion applies.

overview: Offered by Camberwell Grammar School, the General Excellence Scholarship recognises academic merit and exceptional potential. Information regarding the 2027 program will be available shortly.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating academic excellence and overall school achievement. These scholarships are part of the school's commitment to supporting talented students. Scholarships are typically worth between 25% and 66% of the annual fee. Recipients are expected to complete their education at the school and maintain high academic standards.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering various year levels. Age and year level appropriate students. Current school reports and academic performance required.
special_conditions: Recipients must complete education at CGS and maintain high academic achievement. Must participate in school life.

overview: The Music Scholarship recognises musical talent and excellence at Camberwell Grammar School. This scholarship supports students with demonstrated proficiency in musical instruments and performance.
description: The Music Scholarship is designed to support musically talented students who demonstrate exceptional proficiency in their instrument(s). Recipients receive financial support valued between 25% and 66% of annual fees, and may also benefit from music tuition support. The school maintains a strong tradition of musical excellence and is committed to nurturing talented musicians.
eligibility_criteria: Students with demonstrated musical talent and proficiency in primary and secondary instruments. Candidates must meet specified AMEB or equivalent music examination levels. Must be age and year level appropriate.
value_aud: 25-66% of annual fee plus music tuition
special_conditions: Musical audition required. Must provide AMEB or equivalent certification. Photo ID required on testing day.

overview: The Indigenous Scholarship is offered by Camberwell Grammar School to support Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. The school has maintained an indigenous scholarship program for over a decade, enrolling local indigenous students annually.
description: The Indigenous Scholarship supports Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students to access education at Camberwell Grammar School. Since 2011, the school has successfully enrolled indigenous students who participate fully in school life and enrich the community. This scholarship reflects the school's commitment to diversity and cultural recognition. Recipients benefit from financial support valued between 25% and 66% of annual fees.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students. Must demonstrate connection to cultural heritage and alignment with school values. Age and year level appropriate.
special_conditions: Must identify as Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander. Connection to cultural heritage required.

overview: The David de Kretser Scholarship is offered by Camberwell Grammar School to support students who would otherwise be unable to access a Camberwell Grammar School education due to financial circumstances.
description: The David de Kretser Scholarship is designed to provide financial assistance to deserving students who demonstrate financial need. The school has made this scholarship a current focus of their fundraising efforts, recognising the importance of enabling talented students to access quality education regardless of financial circumstances. Recipients receive substantial financial support to cover tuition fees.
eligibility_criteria: Students demonstrating financial need. Must otherwise be able to attend the school academically and through merit. Age and year level appropriate.
special_conditions: Financial need assessment required. Merit-based consideration alongside need.

overview: Academic, General Excellence, Music, Sport, Arts, and Foundation scholarships for various years; Edutest required; 2027 applications open.
Academic, General Excellence, Music, Sport, Arts, and Foundation scholarships for various years; Edutest required; 2027 applications open.

school_name: Caulfield Grammar School - Wheelers Hill Campus
overview: Academic, General Excellence, Music, Sport, Arts, and Foundation scholarships for various years; Edutest required; 2027 applications open.

school_name: Caulfield Grammar School - Yarra Junction Campus
overview: Academic, General Excellence, Music, Sport, Arts, and Foundation scholarships for various years; Edutest required; 2027 applications open.

Chairo Christian School maintains a General Bursary Fund that offers financial assistance to families with identifiable financial need seeking Christian schooling. This is the school's primary financial assistance program, available across all campuses. Applications are made directly through the school's Finance Department.
description: The General Bursary Fund is designed to provide financial support to eligible families who have demonstrated financial need and wish to access a Christian education at Chairo. The bursary assists in the provision of Christian schooling and helps make education more accessible to families across a broad income range. This is the primary financial assistance mechanism available at the school, complementing the school's sibling discount policy and government funding support. More specific information regarding the value, terms, and application process is available from the Finance Department.
eligibility_criteria: Families with identifiable financial need. Applications must be made by parents through the school. Contact the Finance Department for specific eligibility requirements, closing dates, and application forms.
special_conditions: Applications made through Finance Department. Specific eligibility criteria and closing dates available upon inquiry.

overview: Chairo Christian School maintains a General Bursary Fund to assist families with identifiable financial need in accessing Christian education. The program is available to eligible families across all campuses including the Pakenham campus.
description: The school maintains a General Bursary Fund and offers assistance in the provision of Christian schooling to families with an identifiable financial need. More information and application forms are available from the Finance Department. The bursary provides financial assistance to help offset tuition fees for eligible families.
eligibility_criteria: Families with identifiable financial need. Specific income thresholds and requirements determined by the Finance Department.
special_conditions: Families must demonstrate identifiable financial need. Application forms available from Finance Department.

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Camp Macleod
overview: Access Ministries provides encouragement awards to Year 6 students to support the purchase of uniforms or textbooks. These bursaries recognize students selected by the College chaplain in partnership with the primary school principal.
description: Access Ministries provides two encouragement awards for Year 6 students. These bursaries are $200 each and are specifically for the purchase of uniform or textbooks. Students are selected by the College chaplain in partnership with the primary school principal to receive these awards.
eligibility_criteria: Year 6 students. Selection made by College chaplain in partnership with primary school principal.
special_conditions: For purchase of uniform or textbooks only. Selection by chaplain and principal.

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Deaf Facility
overview: Charles La Trobe P-12 College offers academic scholarships to prospective Year 7 students. The College awards five scholarships each year – one for each year level from Year 7 to Year 11. Successful Year 7 scholarship recipients receive a $1000 bursary for the purchase of uniforms or textbooks.
description: Prospective Year 7 students are invited to apply for an academic scholarship of $1000. The College awards five scholarships each year, with one allocated for each secondary year level (Years 7-11). Successful scholarship recipients are provided with a $1000 bursary which can be used toward the purchase of uniforms or textbooks. This program recognizes academic merit and assists families with school-related costs.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective Year 7 students. Specific academic requirements and selection criteria are determined by the College.
special_conditions: Applications close in August of each year

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Deaf Facility
overview: The Lions Club provides a $1000 bursary for Year 10 students whose families experience financial disadvantage. This bursary is designed to assist with the costs associated with transitioning to VCE studies.
description: The Lions Club provides a $1000 bursary for a Year 10 student whose family experiences financial disadvantage to assist with the costs of VCE. This program recognizes families facing financial hardship and provides direct financial support to help offset the expenses of the VCE year, allowing students to continue their secondary education without financial barriers.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 student whose family experiences financial disadvantage. Specific application requirements and assessment criteria are determined by the Lions Club and the College.
special_conditions: Based on financial need assessment. Provided by Lions Club partnership.

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Olympic Village Primary Campus
overview: Charles La Trobe P-12 College offers an academic scholarship to prospective Year 7 students. The scholarship is valued at $1000 and is designed to recognise academic achievement.
description: Prospective Year 7 students are invited to apply for an academic scholarship valued at $1000. The scholarship provides financial support to recognised high-achieving students entering Year 7 at Charles La Trobe P-12 College. Applications close in August each year.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective Year 7 students with demonstrated academic achievement.

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Olympic Village Primary Campus
overview: Lions Club provides a $1000 bursary to a Year 10 student whose family experiences financial disadvantage. The bursary is designed to assist with the costs of VCE preparation.
description: The Lions Club bursary is a $1000 one-off payment provided to a Year 10 student at Charles La Trobe P-12 College whose family is experiencing financial disadvantage. The bursary aims to assist with costs associated with transitioning to VCE studies in Year 11.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students whose families experience financial disadvantage.
special_conditions: Family must be experiencing financial disadvantage.

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Pavilion - The Edge South Morang Campus
overview: Charles La Trobe P-12 College awards five academic scholarships each year—one for each year level from Year 7 to Year 11. The Year 7 scholarship is valued at $1000 and recognizes academic achievement of prospective secondary students entering the college.
description: The Year 7 Academic Scholarship is awarded to prospective Year 7 students demonstrating strong academic achievement. The scholarship is valued at $1000 and is one of five scholarships awarded annually across years 7-11. This scholarship supports talented students commencing their secondary education at the college.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective Year 7 students applying to Charles La Trobe P-12 College. Academic merit required.

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Pavilion - The Edge South Morang Campus
overview: The Masonic Club provides a $500 bursary for a Year 11 student whose family experiences financial disadvantage to help with the costs of Year 12 studies.
description: The Masonic Club Year 11 Bursary is a financial assistance program targeting Year 11 students from families experiencing financial hardship. The bursary provides $500 to assist with Year 12 costs. The secondary school principal selects the recipient based on demonstrated financial need.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 students whose family experiences financial disadvantage.
special_conditions: Principal nomination only - recipient selected by secondary school principal

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-Pavilion - The Edge South Morang Campus
overview: The Lions Club provides a $1000 bursary for a Year 10 student whose family experiences financial disadvantage to assist with the costs of VCE preparation.
description: The Lions Club Year 10 Bursary is a financial assistance program for Year 10 students from families experiencing financial hardship. The bursary provides $1000 to help with VCE-related costs as students transition to Year 11. Recipients receive funds to support their educational progression to VCE studies.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 students whose family experiences financial disadvantage.
special_conditions: For students whose family experiences financial disadvantage

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-The Pavilion Campus - Preston East
overview: Prospective Year 7 students are invited to apply for an academic scholarship of $1000. This scholarship recognises academic achievement in Year 6 students transitioning to secondary education.
description: Prospective Year 7 students are eligible to apply for an academic scholarship worth $1000. The successful students receive a $1000 bursary for the purchase of uniform or text books to support their transition to secondary education at Charles La Trobe P-12 College.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective Year 7 students entering secondary education
special_conditions: Funds designated for uniform or text books

school_name: Charles La Trobe P-12 College-The Pavilion Campus - Preston East
overview: Lions Club provides a $1000 bursary for a Year 10 student whose family experiences financial disadvantage. This bursary assists with the costs associated with transitioning to VCE studies.
description: The Lions Club provides a $1000 bursary specifically for a Year 10 student whose family is experiencing financial disadvantage. This support is designed to assist with the costs of transitioning to VCE in Year 11, removing financial barriers to educational progression.
eligibility_criteria: Year 10 student whose family experiences financial disadvantage
special_conditions: Family must demonstrate financial disadvantage

overview: Christian College Geelong offers Annual Academic Excellence Scholarships to reward students demonstrating outstanding academic achievement. Scholarships are available for entry at multiple year levels across the college's campuses and are open to both current and prospective students.
description: Academic Excellence Scholarships are offered annually at Christian College Geelong to recognize students with exceptional academic achievement. These scholarships are available for entry at Year 5, Year 7, Year 10 and Year 11 levels. Applications are open to both students currently enrolled at Christian College and those attending other schools. The scholarship examinations are held on designated dates, typically in October, with applications closing in September. Scholarship recipients are selected based on their performance in the entrance examination.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students currently in Years 3-10 depending on year level of scholarship application. Students currently attending Christian College are eligible to apply, as well as students from other schools. Applications are accepted for entry in 2026 and 2027.
special_conditions: Scholarship examinations held on Saturday at Christian College campus. Open to both current and prospective students.

school_name: Christian College Geelong - Junior School – Belmont
overview: Christian College Geelong offers Music Excellence Scholarships designed to support students who demonstrate outstanding performance skills and a dedicated commitment to music. This esteemed scholarship recognizes and nurtures passionate student musicians across the College's renowned music program.
description: This scholarship is designed to support the development of outstanding performance skills in students who are passionate about music. Christian College believes in the transformative power of music and the importance of nurturing the gifts and ambition of dedicated, committed musicians. The program offers solo and ensemble performance opportunities, academic and career pathways, and meaningful opportunities for students to engage with each other and the community. Many graduate musicians from the College have gone on to study tertiary music and perform or work across the industry both nationally and internationally.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 5-12 in 2027/2028.
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12 or Multiple
special_conditions: Students must demonstrate outstanding performance skills and commitment to music development.

school_name: Christian College Geelong - Junior School – Belmont
overview: The Salvation Army Music Scholarship, offered in partnership with the Salvation Army's programs such as 'Just Brass', identifies young people from disadvantaged family circumstances and socio-economic situations who would ordinarily be unable to attend Christian College. This scholarship extends educational opportunities to talented musicians from underserved communities.
description: This scholarship is part of Christian College Geelong's foundation programs supporting students from disadvantaged circumstances. In partnership with the Salvation Army and their music programs such as 'Just Brass', the scholarship identifies young people from family circumstances and socio-economic situations that would ordinarily preclude them from enrolling at Christian College. The scholarship provides enrolment opportunities and support for music education at the College.
eligibility_criteria: Young people from disadvantaged family circumstances and socio-economic situations who demonstrate talent in music and would otherwise be unable to afford tuition at Christian College.
special_conditions: Partnership with The Salvation Army required. Students must demonstrate financial need and music aptitude.

school_name: Christian College Geelong - Junior School – Belmont
overview: The Cultural Scholarship extends educational opportunities to young people from refugee backgrounds through partnerships with local churches. This scholarship recognizes the value of cultural diversity and provides support for students who bring unique perspectives and experiences to the College community.
description: Through partnerships with local churches, The Cultural Scholarship extends the opportunity for young people from refugee backgrounds to join the Christian College Geelong family. The scholarship is designed to support students from diverse cultural backgrounds who would otherwise face barriers to accessing independent school education. Recipients bring an incredibly generous spirit, joy and heart to the life of the College community. The Foundation supports the Cultural Scholarship Program by funding a Resource and Community Liaison Officer who works directly with students and their families to break down cultural barriers and provide ongoing support.
eligibility_criteria: Young people from refugee backgrounds. Applications typically made through partnerships with local churches. Students must have refugee status or come from refugee-background families.
special_conditions: Refugee background required. Application through local church partnerships. Support provided by Resource and Community Liaison Officer.

school_name: Christian College Geelong - Junior School – Belmont
overview: Christian College Geelong offers Sponsored Student Placements through its bursary program to assist students from disadvantaged circumstances in maintaining a stable educational experience. This program has been a longstanding commitment of the College to support worthy students regardless of their financial circumstances.
description: The Sponsored Student Placements bursary program provides ongoing financial assistance to help students maintain a stable, educational experience even when life presents unexpected challenges. For many years, Christian College Geelong has made provision to support students from disadvantaged circumstances by providing them with enrolment opportunities. Through this bursary program, students can continue their education at the College despite financial hardship. The College's commitment to this program demonstrates its belief that education should be accessible to all deserving students.
eligibility_criteria: Students from disadvantaged circumstances requiring financial assistance to maintain continuous enrolment. Must demonstrate financial need and commitment to their education.
special_conditions: Application likely through school administration. Ongoing support available for students facing financial difficulties.

overview: The General Excellence Scholarship is offered to current Year 6 students seeking entry to Year 7 at Cornish College. This scholarship recognizes academic excellence and critical thinking ability, requiring applicants to demonstrate strong academic competence through a rigorous selection process.
description: This scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating general academic excellence. Applicants must pass a critical thinking test and participate in an interview with the Principal. The selection process involves an Expression of Interest followed by a shortlist determination, testing on Saturday 2 March 2024, and subsequent interviews. The scholarship recognizes overall academic achievement and potential.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 6 students not currently attending Cornish College who are seeking entry in Year 7. Applicants must demonstrate high level of competence in academic achievement and pass the critical thinking test.
special_conditions: Expression of Interest required to register. Shortlisting process before testing. Interview with Principal required.

overview: The Robert Johnson Scholarship provides a Cornish College education for students who would not otherwise have the financial means to attend. Available to current Year 6 students for entry to Year 7, this scholarship recognizes financial need as the primary criterion while requiring demonstrated academic competence.
description: This need-based scholarship provides financial assistance to enable students from disadvantaged backgrounds to access a Cornish College education. While primarily focused on financial need (families must participate in a means test process), applicants must also demonstrate a high level of competence in academic achievement. The selection process includes a critical thinking test and interview with the Principal.
eligibility_criteria: Current Year 6 students not currently attending Cornish College seeking entry in Year 7. Primary eligibility criterion is financial need, verified through a means test process. Applicants must demonstrate high level of academic competence and pass the critical thinking test.
special_conditions: Means test required to verify financial need. Expression of Interest required to register. Alternate testing date may be offered if student applies for both scholarships.

overview: The Academic Pathway of the Elevate Pathways Program supports students who demonstrate strong academic potential, intellectual curiosity, and a commitment to learning. This program is designed for students entering Year 7 and Year 10 in 2027 and includes a tuition fee reduction for successful applicants.
description: The Elevate Pathways Program recognises potential early and provides a framework to nurture it deliberately. While the Program includes a tuition fee reduction for successful applicants, Elevate is much broader than traditional scholarships. The Academic Pathway supports students who demonstrate strong academic potential, intellectual curiosity, and a commitment to learning. Participants may access mentoring and academic guidance, enrichment opportunities to extend learning, early VCE opportunities where appropriate, and support for relevant study.
eligibility_criteria: Students demonstrating strong academic potential, intellectual curiosity, and commitment to learning entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027.
special_conditions: Program includes mentorship and structured extension designed to support enrichment and personal growth

overview: The Arts Pathway of the Elevate Pathways Program is designed for students who are deepening artistic craft and demonstrate commitment to an artistic discipline. This program is available for students entering Year 7 and Year 10 in 2027 and includes a tuition fee reduction.
description: The Elevate Pathways Program recognises potential early and provides a framework to nurture it deliberately. The Arts Pathway is designed for students already demonstrating commitment to an artistic discipline, deepening artistic craft through mentorship and structured extension. The Program includes a tuition fee reduction for successful applicants along with enrichment experiences and opportunities to extend learning.
eligibility_criteria: Students demonstrating commitment to an artistic discipline entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027.
special_conditions: Program includes mentorship and structured extension focused on deepening artistic craft

overview: The Sport Pathway of the Elevate Pathways Program is designed for students balancing high-level sport with academic and personal growth. This program is available for students entering Year 7 and Year 10 in 2027 and includes a tuition fee reduction.
description: The Elevate Pathways Program recognises potential early and provides a framework to nurture it deliberately. The Sport Pathway supports students already demonstrating commitment to a sporting discipline, seeking to balance high-level athletic pursuit with academic excellence and personal growth. The Program includes a tuition fee reduction for successful applicants along with mentorship and enrichment opportunities to extend learning.
eligibility_criteria: Students demonstrating commitment to a sporting discipline at a high level entering Year 7 or Year 10 in 2027.
special_conditions: Program includes mentorship and structured extension focused on balancing high-level sport with academic and personal growth

school_name: Geelong Grammar School - Bostock House Campus
overview: General Excellence, Academic, Music, Sports, Visual Arts, and Boarding scholarships; up to 75% fee remission; means-tested; 2027 applications open.
General Excellence, Academic, Music, Sports, Visual Arts, and Boarding scholarships; up to 75% fee remission; means-tested; 2027 applications open.
General Excellence, Academic, Music, Sports, Visual Arts, and Boarding scholarships; up to 75% fee remission; means-tested; 2027 applications open.

school_name: Geelong Lutheran College - St John's Newtown Campus
overview: Geelong Lutheran College offers Academic Scholarships to recognise students' gifts and abilities, giving them opportunities to challenge and academically extend themselves. These scholarships are available to prospective and current students entering Year 7 or Year 10. Academic Scholarships provide up to half remission of tuition fees.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to existing and prospective students who demonstrate high academic achievement and are willing to embrace the ethos of the College. The scholarships recognise students' gifts and abilities, providing opportunities for academic extension. Scholarships apply to tuition fees only and do not apply to levies or other costs such as uniforms. For students sitting the Academic Scholarship for Year 10, the tenure of the Scholarship is for Years 10-12. Scholarships are reviewed each semester by the Head of Secondary and are subject to continued excellence in academic performance and adherence to College policies and procedures.
eligibility_criteria: Must be Australian citizens or permanent residents. Existing and prospective students entering Year 7 or Year 10 can apply. All students at GLC are expected to actively participate in activities offered within the College.
special_conditions: Registration fee of $125. Scholarship examination required. Must be enrolled at the College prior to registering for examination.

school_name: Geelong Lutheran College - St John's Newtown Campus
overview: Geelong Lutheran College General Excellence Scholarships acknowledge achievement in Christian faith, leadership and community service, or talent in music, sport, performing arts or visual arts. These scholarships are available to students entering Years 7, 8, 9 or 10.
description: The General Excellence Scholarships recognise and celebrate students who demonstrate dedication, motivation and engagement in a variety of experiences within school and community life. The scholarship acknowledges achievement in the areas of Christian faith, leadership and community service, or talent in music, sport, performing arts or visual arts. These scholarships are open to both external students with an application for 2027 enrolment in the system and currently enrolled Geelong Lutheran College students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Years 7, 8, 9 or 10 at GLC. Includes external students with application for 2027 enrolment and currently enrolled students. Must demonstrate dedication, motivation and engagement in school and community life.
special_conditions: Achievement recognised in Christian faith, leadership, community service, music, sport, performing arts or visual arts.

overview: Individual Excellence Scholarships recognise exceptional talent and achievement across academic, cultural, artistic and leadership domains. Available to new students in Years 8-12, these scholarships strengthen the diversity of the school community. Girton provides specialist academic instruction delivering the region's strongest VCE results alongside excellent pastoral care and extensive co-curricular opportunities.
description: The Individual Excellence Scholarship programme is designed to attract talented students who demonstrate outstanding achievement in any area of school life. These scholarships are open to new students entering Years 8, 9, 10, 11 and 12 (Year 7 is temporarily closed due to demand). The programme recognises that excellence manifests in diverse ways and seeks to celebrate students who shine academically, artistically, culturally or through leadership contributions. Applicants must complete an application form to express their interest.
eligibility_criteria: New students only in Years 8, 9, 10, 11, and 12. Year 7 applications temporarily suspended for 2026 and 2027 due to demand. Applicants must demonstrate exceptional talent or achievement in their chosen area of excellence.
special_conditions: Year 7 applications are NOT accepted for 2026 and 2027 due to demand.

overview: The Academic Scholarship is available to new and current students entering Years 5-11, recognising strong academic performance and potential. This scholarship supports students who demonstrate excellence in their studies and seek to pursue further academic success at Girton.
description: The Academic Scholarship programme is designed for students who demonstrate strong academic achievement and potential. It is available to new students entering Years 5 through 11, as well as current Year 6 Girton students. Applicants must complete a registration form to express their interest in the scholarship. The programme aims to identify and support students who are committed to academic excellence.
eligibility_criteria: New students in Years 5-11 OR current Year 6 Girton students. Academic achievement and demonstrated potential required.
year_levels: Year 5, Year 6, Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11
special_conditions: Current Year 6 Girton students may apply alongside external new student applicants.

overview: Music and Performing Arts Scholarships are available to new and current students entering Years 7-12 in 2027. These scholarships recognise exceptional talent in music and performing arts, and applicants must complete auditions to demonstrate their abilities. The programme seeks to attract students with demonstrated excellence in music performance, drama, dance or related performing arts disciplines.
description: The Music and Performing Arts Scholarship programme celebrates students with exceptional talent in performance-based disciplines. Available to both new and current students entering Years 7 through 12, this scholarship requires applicants to audition and demonstrate their performing arts capabilities. Audition dates and application forms are provided on the school's scholarship page. The programme values the contribution of performing arts to school culture and community engagement.
eligibility_criteria: New and current students entering Years 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, or 12 in 2027. Must demonstrate exceptional talent in music or performing arts through audition.
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Audition required for all applicants. Current students may apply alongside new student applicants.

overview: Girton Grammar's scholarships recognise talent, reward achievement, and open doors to outstanding educational opportunities. The Individual Excellence Scholarship is available to new students and rewards academic merit and achievement.
description: The Individual Excellence Scholarship recognises academic excellence and talent in incoming students. The scholarship is designed to support gifted students and strengthen the diversity of the school community. Applications are made through the school's enrolment process, and successful applicants receive financial support towards their tuition fees.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new students entering Years 8-12 in 2027. Year 7 is closed to new scholarship applications for 2026 and 2027 due to demand. Year 6 applications are also closed for 2026. Applicants must demonstrate academic excellence and merit.
year_levels: Year 6, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Limited availability due to demand. Year 7 not accepting applications for 2026 and 2027.

overview: Girton Grammar offers Music Scholarships to recognise and support musical talent. These scholarships are available to new and current students entering Years 7-12 in 2027.
description: Music Scholarships are designed to support students with demonstrated musical talent and ability. The school provides specialist music instruction as part of its co-curricular program. Applicants must audition to demonstrate their musical proficiency and potential.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and current students entering Years 7-12 in 2027. Applicants must demonstrate musical talent through audition.
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Audition required. Application forms and audition dates available on the scholarships page.

overview: Girton Grammar offers Performing Arts Scholarships to recognise and support performing arts talent. These scholarships are available to new and current students entering Years 7-12 in 2027.
description: Performing Arts Scholarships support students demonstrating excellence in drama, dance, or other performing arts disciplines. The school has an extensive performing arts program as part of its co-curricular offerings. Applicants must audition to demonstrate their performing arts ability.
eligibility_criteria: Open to new and current students entering Years 7-12 in 2027. Applicants must demonstrate performing arts talent through audition.
year_levels: Year 7, Year 8, Year 9, Year 10, Year 11, Year 12
special_conditions: Audition required. Application forms and audition dates available on the scholarships page.

overview: Good News Lutheran College offers Academic Scholarships at the Tarneit Campus for entry to Year 7, Year 9 and Year 11 and Mambourin Campus for Year 7 only. Academic Scholarships are open to all students, including current GNLC students and those not yet enrolled at the College.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded based on an academic examination using objective measures provided by the Australian Council for Educational Research (ACER). Scholarship recipients can receive a reduction of 20%-50% on annual tuition fees for up to two years, provided they meet the Academic Scholarship conditions. These scholarships recognize academic achievement and provide financial support to deserving students.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all students, including current GNLC students and those not yet enrolled at the College. Must be entering Year 7, Year 9 or Year 11 at Tarneit Campus; or Year 7 at Mambourin Campus.
special_conditions: Scholarship covers up to two years and recipients must meet ongoing Academic Scholarship conditions.

school_name: Good News Lutheran College - Mambourin Campus
overview: Good News Lutheran College offers Academic Scholarships for entry to Year 7 at both Tarneit and Mambourin campuses. These scholarships are open to all students, including current GNLC students, recognising academic excellence and achievement.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate academic excellence. The scholarships provide fee reductions for successful applicants. Year 7 entry scholarships are available at both the Tarneit and Mambourin campuses. The program supports student success through tuition fee reductions for high-achieving students.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7 students seeking entry to the college. Open to all students, including current Good News Lutheran College students. Academic merit required. Specific assessment criteria determined by the college.
special_conditions: Available at Mambourin Campus for Year 7 entry only

school_name: Good News Lutheran College - Mambourin Campus
overview: Good News Lutheran College offers Academic Scholarships for entry to Year 9 at the Tarneit Campus. These scholarships are open to all students, including current GNLC students, recognising academic excellence and achievement.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate academic excellence. The scholarships provide fee reductions for successful applicants. Year 9 entry scholarships are available at the Tarneit campus. The program supports student success through tuition fee reductions for high-achieving students.
eligibility_criteria: Year 9 students seeking entry to the college. Open to all students, including current Good News Lutheran College students. Academic merit required. Specific assessment criteria determined by the college.
special_conditions: Available at Tarneit Campus only

school_name: Good News Lutheran College - Mambourin Campus
overview: Good News Lutheran College offers Academic Scholarships for entry to Year 11 at the Tarneit Campus. These scholarships are open to all students, including current GNLC students, recognising academic excellence and achievement.
description: Academic Scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate academic excellence. The scholarships provide fee reductions for successful applicants. Year 11 entry scholarships are available at the Tarneit campus. The program supports student success through tuition fee reductions for high-achieving students.
eligibility_criteria: Year 11 students seeking entry to the college. Open to all students, including current Good News Lutheran College students. Academic merit required. Specific assessment criteria determined by the college.
special_conditions: Available at Tarneit Campus only

overview: Offers Academic, Excellence, and Foundation scholarships for Years 7, 9, 10; means-tested options available; applications via online form.
Offers Academic, Excellence, and Foundation scholarships for Years 7, 9, 10; means-tested options available; applications via online form.
