Inaugural means-tested scholarship program for Haileybury Pangea students launched in 2026. Designed to empower students from rural, regional and diverse communities by breaking down barriers to high-quality online education. Supports students who would not ordinarily have the opportunity to afford a Haileybury Pangea education.
description: The Haileybury Pangea Foundation Scholarship is a life-changing opportunity that eliminates geographical barriers for students in rural and regional areas. The scholarship supports students who might not have access to high-quality education due to their location, giving them the same opportunities as those in more accessible areas. Recipients benefit from a supportive, structured environment with strong wellbeing ethos, flexibility to design their educational journey, and comprehensive social and wellbeing programs focusing on resilience, character development, and social justice. This inaugural program represents a unique opportunity to be part of a pioneering initiative that sets a precedent for future scholarships at Haileybury Pangea.
eligibility_criteria: Open to Victorian students in Year 7 and above. Must be an Australian citizen or permanent resident of Victoria. Would not ordinarily have the opportunity to afford a Haileybury Pangea education based on financial circumstances. Shortlisted candidates will be interviewed by Head of Campus and a member of the Foundation's Board.
special_conditions: Limited number of scholarships available. Launching in 2026. Requires parent/guardian at home during study. Must reside in Victoria or Northern Territory.

overview: Heritage College is offering an Academic Scholarship awarded to students who excel academically. This scholarship recognises outstanding academic achievement for students entering Year 7 or Year 10.
description: The Academic Scholarship is offered to prospective and current students who demonstrate exceptional academic performance. It is available for entry into Year 7 in 2026 and for current students entering Year 10 in 2026. The scholarship recognises and rewards academic excellence and student achievement.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective and current students entering Year 7 in 2026, and current students entering Year 10 in 2026.

overview: Heritage College offers academic scholarships to students who excel academically. The scholarship program recognises student excellence across academic areas, and recipients must commit to supporting the college's Christian ethos while contributing to college life.
description: Heritage College's Academic Scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating advanced academic standing. The program recognises excellence including academic achievement, and scholarships are offered on a three-year basis commencing on the year of entry. The scholarship is awarded based on examination results, school reports, NAPLAN reports and interviews. Heritage College offers a limited number of academic scholarships to prospective and enrolled students each year.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective students entering Year 7 or current students entering Year 10 who demonstrate advanced academic standing are encouraged to apply. Students must commit to the college's Christian ethos.
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis. Recipients must support the college's Christian ethos.

overview: Heritage College offers sporting scholarships to students who excel in particular sporting disciplines. The program recognises sporting excellence and recipients must commit to supporting the college's Christian ethos.
description: Heritage College's Sporting Scholarship recognises students with excellence in particular sporting disciplines. Students with advanced sporting achievement are encouraged to apply. Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis commencing on the year of entry. Awards are based on examination results, school reports, NAPLAN reports and interviews.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective students entering Year 7 or current students entering Year 10 who excel in a particular sporting discipline are encouraged to apply. Must commit to the college's Christian ethos.
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis. Recipients must support the college's Christian ethos.

overview: Heritage College offers leadership scholarships to students who demonstrate leadership in community service. The program recognises community leadership excellence and recipients must commit to the college's Christian ethos.
description: Heritage College's Leadership Scholarship recognises students who demonstrate leadership in community service. Prospective Year 7 students who demonstrate leadership in community service are encouraged to apply. Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis commencing on the year of entry. Awards are based on examination results, school reports, NAPLAN reports and interviews.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective students entering Year 7 or current students entering Year 10 who demonstrate leadership in community service are encouraged to apply. Must commit to the college's Christian ethos.
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis. Recipients must support the college's Christian ethos.

overview: Heritage College offers scholarships to students who demonstrate creative excellence. The program recognises creative arts achievement and recipients must commit to supporting the college's Christian ethos.
description: Heritage College's Creative Arts Scholarship recognises students with creative excellence. Students with advanced creative standing are encouraged to apply. Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis commencing on the year of entry. Awards are based on examination results, school reports, NAPLAN reports and interviews.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective students entering Year 7 or current students entering Year 10 who demonstrate creative excellence are encouraged to apply. Must commit to the college's Christian ethos.
special_conditions: Scholarships are offered on a three-year basis. Recipients must support the college's Christian ethos.

overview: Full and Half scholarships for up to six years are awarded to high achieving Year 7 applicants. Heritage College Knox recognises outstanding academic achievement and provides substantial financial support to exceptional students entering Year 7.
description: Heritage College Knox offers Full and Half scholarships for high achieving students entering Year 7. Scholarships cover general tuition fees and are available for up to six years of study. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to tuition, ensemble and performance activities. All scholarships exclude excursions, camps, swimming and extra-curricular activities.
eligibility_criteria: Students who will be in Year 7 in 2025. High achieving applicants. Must be willing to demonstrate commitment to tuition, ensemble and performance.
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Successful recipients must register at scholarships.acer.edu.au and attend HCK on designated test date (24 February 2024 referenced).

overview: Full and Half scholarships for up to two years are awarded to high achieving Year 10 applicants. Heritage College Knox supports exceptional students continuing their secondary education with substantial financial assistance.
description: Heritage College Knox offers Full and Half scholarships for high achieving students entering Year 10. Scholarships cover general tuition fees and are available for up to two years of study. Recipients must demonstrate commitment to tuition, ensemble and performance activities. All scholarships exclude excursions, camps, swimming and extra-curricular activities.
eligibility_criteria: Students who will be in Year 10 in 2025. High achieving applicants. Must be willing to demonstrate commitment to tuition, ensemble and performance.
value_aud: Full Fee Remission or 50% Fee Reduction
special_conditions: Successful recipients must register at scholarships.acer.edu.au and attend HCK on designated test date.

school_name: Kardinia International College - Kardinia Grove Campus
overview: Kardinia International College offers Music Scholarships for Years 7-11 students who demonstrate excellence in instrumental or vocal performance. These scholarships recognise significant musical achievement and require participation in College music ensembles as a condition of the award.
description: The Music Scholarships are awarded to students entering Years 7-11 who demonstrate exceptional performance on their primary instrument. Successful applicants are required to participate in instrumental and/or vocal ensembles as deemed appropriate by the College. Recipients are entitled to a reduction in academic and/or music tuition fees as determined by the Principal. Scholarships are reviewed biennially. All instrumental music fees remain the responsibility of the recipient.
eligibility_criteria: Year 7-11 entry. Applicants must demonstrate high musical achievement in instrumental or vocal performance. Students must be able to commit to participation in a music ensemble. Submission of video audition required.
special_conditions: Successful applicants must participate in an instrumental and/or vocal ensemble. Scholarships reviewed biennially. All instrumental music fees remain recipient's responsibility.

school_name: Kardinia International College - Kardinia Grove Campus
overview: From 2027, Kardinia International College will offer Bridges Scholarships to Years 7-11 students who demonstrate general excellence and could not otherwise attend the College for financial reasons. These scholarships recognise achievement across academic, arts, sport, leadership, STEM, community service and social justice.
description: The Bridges Scholarships are awarded for general excellence with emphasis on academic achievement combined with high achievement in at least one of the following areas: the arts, sport, leadership, science and technology, community service or social justice. Winners must demonstrate a strong connection to the College's cornerstones, particularly international mindedness. The awarding process involves an initial application followed by a panel interview for shortlisted students. Financial need is a key consideration for eligibility.
eligibility_criteria: New students entering Years 7-11 in 2027 or later. Must demonstrate general excellence with particular emphasis on academic achievement plus high achievement in at least one of: the arts, sport, leadership, science and technology, community service, or social justice. Must demonstrate strong connection to College's cornerstones, especially international mindedness. Must be unable to otherwise attend the College due to financial circumstances.
special_conditions: Available from 2027 onwards for new entrant Year 7-11 students only. Selection based on application followed by panel interview for shortlisted candidates.

overview: Academic scholarships awarded on the basis of an independent academic examination and an interview. Available for entry to Year 5, Year 7, Year 9, and Year 11, offered at the Principal's discretion.
description: Academic Excellence Scholarships are merit-based awards designed to recognize outstanding academic achievement. Successful applicants are required to fully participate in school life through to the end of Year 12. If a scholarship student chooses to leave the School before the completion of Year 12, the value of the scholarships must be repaid in full.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5, Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11. Must perform well in independent academic examination and interview. No additional requirements for Academic Excellence Scholarship applicants.
special_conditions: Acceptance and fees payable required within two business days of offer. If scholarship holder leaves before Year 12, full repayment required.

overview: Music scholarships awarded on the basis of an independent academic examination, an interview and an audition. Recognizes musical excellence and performance ability across multiple year levels.
description: Music Scholarships are designed for students demonstrating significant musical ability and potential. Scholarships are awarded based on academic examination results, interview performance, and a musical audition. Applicants must meet minimum AMEB (Australian Music Examinations Board) grade requirements depending on year level of entry. Successful applicants are required to fully participate in school life through to the end of Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Minimum AMEB Grade 4 level for Year 5 entry, AMEB Grade 5 level for Year 7 entry, AMEB Grade 6 level for Year 9 entry. Available for Year 5, 7, 9, and 11 entry. Must pass academic examination, interview, and audition.
special_conditions: Minimum AMEB grade levels required. Musical audition component required. Acceptance and fees payable required within two business days of offer.

overview: General Excellence Scholarships recognize outstanding achievement across academic, sporting, arts and leadership domains. Available for students entering multiple year levels.
description: General Excellence Scholarships are awarded to students who demonstrate exceptional ability and potential across a range of areas including academics, sports, performing arts, visual arts, and leadership. These scholarships recognize well-rounded students who make significant contributions to school life. Successful applicants must fully participate in school life through to the end of Year 12.
eligibility_criteria: Open to students entering Year 5, Year 7, Year 9, or Year 11 demonstrating excellence in multiple domains (academic, sporting, arts, or leadership). Must pass academic examination and interview.
special_conditions: Acceptance and fees payable required within two business days of offer. If scholarship holder leaves before Year 12, full repayment required.

overview: Academic, General Excellence, Foundation, and Arts scholarships; means-tested; 2027 applications open late October.

Lighthouse Christian College offers merit-based scholarships to prospective students who meet academic and other eligibility criteria. Up to 50% fee discount is available, tenable until the end of Year 12.
description: The Lighthouse Scholarship provides financial support to academically talented students. Recipients can receive up to 50% fee discount on school fees, which is maintained throughout their secondary education until the end of Year 12. The scholarship recognises academic excellence and supports qualifying students in accessing a Christian education. Selection is based on performance on the ACER Scholarship Test.
eligibility_criteria: Prospective and current students applying for the scholarship must submit a complete enrolment application along with the scholarship registration form. Academic merit is assessed through the ACER Scholarship Test. Applicants are ranked on their test performance.
special_conditions: Scholarship is tenable until end of Year 12 with continued satisfactory performance. Registration fee of $150 is non-refundable.

overview: Lighthouse Christian College offers bursaries to support families experiencing financial difficulty. These bursaries may support educational programs including university enhancement programs while students are in Year 12.
description: The College's bursary program provides financial assistance to families who demonstrate genuine financial need. Bursaries can support a range of educational initiatives, including university enhancement programs for Year 12 students to help prepare them for tertiary education. The bursary program demonstrates the College's commitment to ensuring all capable students can access a quality Christian education regardless of financial circumstances.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial difficulty are eligible to apply. Year 12 students planning to pursue university education may be eligible for bursaries to support enhancement programs. Application requires submission of financial documentation and supporting evidence of financial need.
special_conditions: Bursaries subject to availability of funds and assessment of financial need by the College.

overview: Little Yarra Steiner School offers scholarships designed to provide support to students who may face financial barriers to accessing an independent Steiner education. The program is open to both new and current students from Class 1 to Year 12 with a 3-year duration.
description: The LYSS Scholarship Program is designed to support students with demonstrated financial need and commitment to Steiner education. The scholarship provides a discount on the student's annual tuition fee, which covers all essential aspects such as camps, excursions, swimming lessons for Classes 2-7, classroom materials, stationery (excluding Class 8 and above which have a booklist), and a weekly individual music lesson from Class 3-8. A separate capital levy charge is applicable. The scholarship is awarded to students who exhibit enthusiasm in their studies, demonstrate resilience in overcoming challenges, excel in their areas of strength, and contribute positively to their class dynamics both academically and socially.
eligibility_criteria: Open to both new and current students from Class 1 to Year 12. Applicants must demonstrate: genuine enthusiasm and curiosity towards learning; willingness to contribute positively to the school community; strong commitment from the family to actively support the student's educational journey within the Steiner educational framework; display of integrity, kindness, and respect towards others; demonstrated potential to excel in a Steiner educational environment, as evidenced by previous work and achievements. One parent must hold a Health Care Card or Pensioner Concession Card. The child must be named as a dependent on the card. The family must maintain concession card status throughout the scholarship period.
special_conditions: Continued eligibility is contingent upon meeting selection criteria. The school will actively engage with families regarding scholarship retention. The school reserves the right to withdraw scholarships where selection criteria are not met. Capital levy charges are separate. Applicants must submit a portfolio of work (external applicants only), school reports (external applicants only), and a cover letter addressing selection criteria. New students must complete an Application for Admission form.

overview: Mansfield Steiner School offers a limited number of Access Scholarships to families for whom fees may present a barrier to attendance. These scholarships are confidentially assessed based on individual financial circumstances.
description: The Access Scholarship program is designed to make Steiner education accessible to families facing financial hardship. Scholarships are confidentially assessed based on individual family financial circumstances. The school is committed to providing education to families from diverse socio-economic backgrounds who are committed to Steiner education. As of 2025, the school has decided to phase out merit-based scholarships and now focuses on financial need-based support only.
eligibility_criteria: Families who face financial hardship where fees may present a barrier to attendance. Families must be committed to Steiner education at Mansfield. Financial circumstances are assessed confidentially.
special_conditions: Scholarships are confidentially assessed. Limited places available. School has phased out merit-based scholarships as of 2025.

overview: The Jennifer Munyard Scholarship provides girls and boys who have experienced significant trauma the opportunity to be educated in a safe learning environment at Maranatha Christian School. The purpose is to provide financial support to students who have experienced significant trauma and would not otherwise be able to afford a Christian education. The school has limited places available for this scholarship.
description: The Jennifer Munyard Scholarship is designed specifically for students who have experienced significant trauma. The scholarship aims to provide financial support to eligible students who would not otherwise be able to afford a Christian education at Maranatha Christian School. The selection panel assesses each application according to established criteria. The school notes that only limited scholarship places are available each year. This scholarship is part of the school's broader outreach and support for vulnerable families.
eligibility_criteria: Students who have experienced significant trauma. Selection is based on demonstrated financial need and ability to benefit from a safe learning environment at the school. Applications are assessed by a selection panel according to established criteria.
special_conditions: Applicants must have experienced significant trauma. Limited places available annually.

overview: Designed to recognize and maintain high academic achievement for Year 7 entry students. This scholarship supports students who have demonstrated strong academic performance and commits them to continue pursuing excellence throughout their secondary education at Marymede Catholic College.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is intended to maintain and improve the high standard of academic achievement of students and to give them the opportunity to continue to excel in their future years. The scholarship carries remission of up to 25% of tuition fees and is conditional upon sound academic progress and active participation in all aspects of College life. The scholarship is in effect for the student's six years in Secondary School at the College subject to continued high academic progress. All scholarships are subject to annual review and the College reserves the right to withdraw any Scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress and/or participation in College life.
eligibility_criteria: Must be either a Year 6 student at Marymede Catholic College or a prospective external student applying for Year 7 enrolment. Students will be required to sit the Scholarship Test along with a formal interview for shortlisted candidates.
special_conditions: Conditional upon the recipient remaining at Marymede Catholic College until the end of Year 12, except for relocation to another state. All scholarships are subject to annual review.

overview: Recognizes students who demonstrate excellence across multiple areas including academics, leadership, contributions to their current school or community, personal qualities, and special talents. Available to Year 7 entry students.
description: General Excellence Scholarships are assessed by application by the Principal, taking five categories into consideration. The scholarship carries remission of up to 25% of tuition fees and is conditional upon students meeting the eligibility requirements and College expectations. This scholarship would be in effect for the student's six years in Secondary School at the College. Students will be required to sit the Scholarship Test with a formal interview following for any students who are shortlisted into Stage 2.
eligibility_criteria: Must be either a current Year 6 student at Marymede Catholic College or a prospective external student applying for enrolment at Marymede Catholic College. Candidates are assessed on five categories: academic achievement, leadership qualities, personal qualities, contributions to school/current school, and special talents.
special_conditions: Conditional upon the recipient remaining at Marymede Catholic College until the end of Year 12, except for relocation to another state. All scholarships are subject to annual review and the College reserves the right to withdraw any Scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress and/or participation in College life.

overview: Available to students entering Year 7, this scholarship recognises high academic achievement. It is intended to maintain and improve the high standard of academic achievement and give students the opportunity to excel in their future years at the College.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is offered to Year 6 students at Marymede Catholic College or prospective external students applying for Year 7 enrolment. The scholarship is assessed through a competitive selection process and is intended to maintain and improve the high standard of academic achievement. Recipients may receive fee remission of up to 25% of tuition fees. The scholarship would be in effect for the student's six years in Secondary School at the College, subject to continued high academic progress.
eligibility_criteria: Must be either a current Year 6 student at Marymede Catholic College or a prospective external student applying for Year 7 enrolment. Candidates must sit the Scholarship Test and may be required to participate in a formal interview if shortlisted.
special_conditions: Scholarship subject to annual review. College reserves the right to withdraw any Scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress and/or participation in College life. Conditional upon recipient remaining at Marymede until end of Year 12, except for relocation to another state.

overview: Available to Year 6 students at Marymede or external prospective students entering Year 7, this scholarship recognises excellence across multiple categories including academic achievement, leadership, and contribution to the college community.
description: The General Excellence Scholarship is assessed by application by the Principal, taking five categories into consideration including academic achievement, leadership, sporting or artistic achievement, and contribution to the college/current school. Recipients may receive fee remission of up to 25% of tuition fees. The scholarship is in effect for the student's six years in Secondary School at the College. Selection is based on strength of application, with interviews for shortlisted candidates.
eligibility_criteria: Must be either a current Year 6 student at Marymede Catholic College or a prospective external student applying for Year 7 enrolment. Students must sit the Scholarship Test with a formal interview following for any students shortlisted into Stage 2.
special_conditions: Scholarship subject to annual review. College reserves the right to withdraw any Scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress and/or participation in College life. Conditional upon recipient remaining at Marymede until end of Year 12, except for relocation to another state.

overview: Available to Year 7 students, this scholarship recognises excellence in music performance and provides support for music education through remission of instrumental music lesson fees.
description: The Music Scholarship may carry full remission up to 100% on the Instrumental Music lesson fees of the primary instrument studied. This Scholarship is conditional upon students meeting the eligibility requirements and College expectations. The scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating high achievement in music and is assessed through application and audition process.
eligibility_criteria: Available to prospective students applying for Year 7 enrolment at Marymede Catholic College. Candidates must demonstrate achievement in music performance and meet the College's eligibility requirements.
value_aud: Up to 100% Instrumental Music Lesson Fee Remission
special_conditions: Scholarship subject to annual review. College reserves the right to withdraw any Scholarship in the event of unsatisfactory academic progress and/or participation in College life. Conditional upon recipient remaining at Marymede until end of Year 12, except for relocation to another state.

overview: The Mentone Grammar Community Scholarship provides life-changing educational opportunities from Years 9–12. This fully funded scholarship is open to students with talents across academic, service, creative, sporting, and/or leadership areas who demonstrate significant potential to contribute meaningfully to the school community.
description: The Community Scholarship is a fully funded, means-tested scholarship that covers 100% of tuition fees and other charges as per the Mentone Grammar Schedule of Fees from Years 9–12. The scholarship also covers all levies, uniform costs, one laptop, school transport, all books, compulsory sports equipment, educational sundries and incidentals. The scholarship is generously supported by a current school family with an enduring three-generation connection to Mentone Grammar. It aims to provide access to exceptional students who would otherwise be unable to access a Mentone Grammar education. The scholarship reflects the school's values of Caring, Discipline, Endeavour, Integrity, Resilience, Respect and Service.
eligibility_criteria: New students only; Australian Citizens not currently enrolled, attending, previously attended, or waitlisted at Mentone Grammar. Applicants must be enrolled in a Victorian secondary school in Year 8 at time of application, with intention to enter Year 9 the following year. Must have attended an Australian primary school from Year 3. Must reside within reasonable distance of school with access to Mentone Grammar private bus service or public transport. Must demonstrate sound academic foundation and consistently achieved State average for NAPLAN results. Must show financial need and demonstrate how access would not be possible without this support. Applicants must demonstrate alignment with school values and commitment to community engagement.
special_conditions: Shortlisted applicants participate in interview with Scholarship Selection Committee in week commencing 15 June 2026. Families responsible for specific school items including school shoes, sports shoes, private music/drama tuition, and non-compulsory camps/study tours. Student must remain enrolled until Year 12 completion. Student must maintain sound schoolwork record and active participation in sport and school activities. Repayment of all scholarship funds required if student leaves before end of Year 12 unless Principal approves otherwise.

overview: Merbein P-10 College offers scholarships for Year 7 entry to students with strong academic capabilities. Two scholarships are awarded annually, each valued at $350, based on overall academic performance.
description: This scholarship is awarded to students demonstrating strong academic capabilities. It recognises excellence in academic performance. The scholarship is valued at $350 and is paid as a Merbein P-10 voucher that can be used for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 at Merbein P-10 College. Applicants must demonstrate strong academic capabilities on the basis of overall academic performance.
special_conditions: Voucher format - redeemable for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees

description: This scholarship recognises students who demonstrate high levels of participation in a range of school and community activities and are positive role models to their peers. Recipients show commitment to community engagement and leadership. The scholarship is valued at $350 and is paid as a Merbein P-10 voucher that can be used for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 at Merbein P-10 College. Applicants must demonstrate high levels of participation in school and community activities and be positive role models to their peers.
special_conditions: Voucher format - redeemable for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees

overview: Merbein P-10 College awards one scholarship annually for Year 7 entry to students demonstrating high level sporting prowess, potential and commitment. The scholarship is valued at $350.
description: This scholarship is awarded to students who demonstrate a high level of sporting prowess, potential and commitment to sport. It recognises athletic excellence and dedication to sporting pursuits. The scholarship is valued at $350 and is paid as a Merbein P-10 voucher that can be used for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 at Merbein P-10 College. Applicants must demonstrate a high level of sporting prowess, potential and commitment.
special_conditions: Voucher format - redeemable for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees

The scholarship is valued at $350 and is paid as a Merbein P-10 voucher that can be used for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 7 at Merbein P-10 College. Applicants must demonstrate talent and dedication in music or performing arts.
special_conditions: Voucher format - redeemable for uniforms, camp costs, or school fees

overview: Mount Ridley P-12 College offers academic scholarships funded by business partners to subsidise educational costs. Successful recipients receive funds towards essential educational items for their schooling.
description: This scholarship program is made possible through generous financial support from business partners who are committed to assisting families with educational expenses. The College ensures an equitable selection process where recipients are chosen based on academic merit and potential. Successful applicants receive funds that can be used towards purchasing essential items for their schooling needs.
eligibility_criteria: Open to all P-12 students at Mount Ridley College. Applicants must provide two suitable referees. Selection is made based on academic merit through a shortlisting process conducted by College leadership team members.
special_conditions: Application form must include two suitable referees. Winners are notified via formal notification process.

school_name: Mount Scopus Memorial College - Fink Karp Ivany Campus
overview: Offers needs-based bursaries and grants for Prep to Year 12; music scholarships for Years 7-12; applications anytime via contact.

Mt Hira College provides scholarships to students showing exceptional promise who will act as ambassadors for the college. Secondary scholarships are awarded to students demonstrating outstanding academic ability across core subjects.
description: The Secondary Scholarship programme at Mt Hira College is designed to recognise and support students of exceptional academic merit. Applicants are required to sit an examination consisting of two components: English and Mathematics. The college seeks students who demonstrate exceptional promise and will serve as ambassadors for the institution, contributing to the college community through academic excellence and leadership.
eligibility_criteria: Students applying for secondary scholarship must demonstrate exceptional academic promise. Applicants are required to sit an entrance examination in English and Mathematics. sub_type: General Academic Merit with Excellence in Sport, Music, Leadership or Community Service
overview: Oakleigh Grammar offers scholarships for talented, motivated and civic-minded students who demonstrate potential to make a contribution in leadership and academic capacity. Scholarships are granted to students who display excellence in sport, music, leadership, or community service alongside strong academics. Successful applicants display qualities aligned with the school's values of Humility, Aspiration, Respect and Kindness.
description: Oakleigh Grammar, in conjunction with ACER, seeks to encourage talented students who wish to excel in their learning journey across academic, sport, music, and community domains. Applicants must have a great academic standard and/or excellence in at least one of: sport, music, leadership, or community service. The school recognises curious learners, active sports participants, musically talented students, and those community focused. All scholarship candidates must sit the ACER examination.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 6, 7, 8, 9, or 10 in 2027. Applicants must have good to great academic standard plus excellence in sport, music, leadership, or community service. Must sit ACER examination. Current Oakleigh Grammar students may also apply. Candidates must provide school reports, recent NAPLAN results, certificates of achievement, evidence of sporting/musical/leadership abilities, and may include references from teachers, principals, or community organisations.
special_conditions: Interviews are conducted as part of selection process. Application submitted through ACER portal.

school_name: Overnewton Anglican Community College - Yirramboi Campus
overview: Overnewton Anglican Community College offers Academic Scholarships to outstanding students entering Year 5. These scholarships are awarded on the basis of academic merit and continue through to the completion of Year 12, providing long-term support for students who demonstrate a strong commitment to learning.
description: The College offers a select number of Academic Scholarships to outstanding students entering Year 5. These scholarships are awarded on the basis of academic merit and provide long-term support through to Year 12. The scholarship program is designed to support students who demonstrate academic potential and a strong commitment to learning, and who will contribute positively to the life of the school. Applicants are required to sit an external ACER scholarship examination to ensure equitable assessment based on a reliable and standardised measure of academic ability. The scholarships remain open to both current Overnewton students and those attending other schools.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Year 5 in 2027. Applicants must sit the external ACER Primary Scholarship Test. Open to both current Overnewton students and those attending other schools. Selection based on academic merit and examination performance.
special_conditions: Non-refundable application fee includes access to a practice test with marked answers. Shortlisted applicants only will be contacted for an interview. Scholarship continues through to Year 12.

school_name: Preshil, The Margaret Lyttle Memorial School - Blackhall-Kalimna Preshil Senior School
overview: Means-tested bursaries available; no academic scholarships; focus on inclusive education with financial aid for diverse families.

Scholarships awarded to high achieving students based on academic results during their study at the College and entrance exam results for prospective students. The program recognises and rewards students demonstrating excellence in their academic pursuits.
description: The Academic Excellence Scholarship is offered to both current and prospective students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement. Current students are assessed on their academic results during their study at Sirius College, while prospective students are evaluated based on their entrance exam results. This scholarship aims to reward high achieving students and encourage them in their pursuit of academic excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Secondary level students (Year 7 - Year 12). Current students assessed on academic results; prospective students on entrance exam results. Must demonstrate high academic achievement.
special_conditions: Entrance exam required for prospective students

overview: Scholarships recognising students with special talents in sports, creative/performing arts, music and other abilities. These awards acknowledge exceptional achievement in extracurricular areas beyond academics.
description: Sirius College offers scholarships for students demonstrating exceptional talents in four specific areas: sports, creative/performing arts, music, and other abilities. These scholarships recognise and reward students who show exceptional capability and achievement in these special talent areas. The college supports the development of well-rounded students with diverse abilities beyond academic excellence.
eligibility_criteria: Students must demonstrate exceptional talent in one of the following areas: sports, creative/performing arts, music, or other special abilities. Open to both current and prospective students.
special_conditions: Portfolio, audition or demonstration of talent may be required

overview: Scholarships recognising students who are making significant contributions to their community. The college supports socially responsible individuals motivated by moral values and a belief in tolerance, diversity and harmony.
description: The Community Service Scholarship acknowledges and rewards students who are making significant contributions to their community. Sirius College supports the development of socially responsible individuals who are motivated by moral values and a belief in tolerance, diversity and harmony. This scholarship reflects the college's commitment to fostering community-minded citizens.
eligibility_criteria: Students must demonstrate significant contributions to their community through volunteer work, community engagement or social responsibility initiatives. Must reflect the college's values of tolerance, diversity and harmony.
special_conditions: Evidence of community service contributions required

overview: Scholarships for families experiencing financial hardship due to loss of income through illness or other circumstances beyond their control. Designed to help families meet the college's fee requirements.
description: Sirius College recognises that families can experience both short term and long term financial hardship as a result of the loss of income through illness or other circumstances beyond their control. The Financial Difficulty Scholarship is designed to support families who are experiencing difficulty in meeting the fee requirements of the College, helping to lessen their financial hardships and enable continued education at the college.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship due to loss of income, illness, or circumstances beyond their control. Special consideration for Refugee Financial Difficulty Scholarship (only eligible for subclass 200, 201, 202, 203, 204 and 866 visa holders).
special_conditions: Financial documentation required. Refugee visa holders eligible for separate refugee scholarship stream.

overview: Sirius College offers academic excellence scholarships to reward high-achieving students. These scholarships recognize academic achievement based on entrance exam results for prospective students and academic results for current students.
description: These scholarships aim to reward high achieving students. They are based on students' academic results during their study at the College as well as prospective students according to their entrance exam results. In addition to academic scholarships, VCE awards are available for students who display excellence by receiving a significantly high ATAR or a perfect score in one of their studies.
eligibility_criteria: Current students with strong academic results and prospective students with excellent entrance exam results. VCE award recipients must achieve significantly high ATAR or perfect scores in individual studies.

overview: Sirius College recognizes students making significant contributions to their community through a community service scholarship. This scholarship acknowledges socially responsible individuals motivated by moral values and a belief in tolerance, diversity and harmony.
description: Students who are making significant contributions to their community are acknowledged by a community service scholarship. Sirius College supports socially responsible individuals motivated by moral values and a belief in tolerance, diversity and harmony.
eligibility_criteria: Students demonstrating significant community service contributions and commitment to social responsibility, moral values, tolerance, diversity and harmony.

overview: Sirius College recognizes that families can experience financial hardship and offers financial difficulty scholarships to support families. This scholarship is available to assist with fee requirements for families experiencing temporary or long-term financial challenges.
description: Sirius College recognises that families can experience both short term and long term financial hardship as a result of the loss of income through illness or other circumstances beyond their control. To this extent, families who are experiencing difficulty in meeting the fee requirements of the College can apply for financial difficulty scholarship.
eligibility_criteria: Families experiencing financial hardship due to loss of income through illness or circumstances beyond their control. Refugee scholarship available for eligible visa subclasses (200, 201, 202, 203, 204, 866).
special_conditions: Refugee visa holders must hold eligible visa subclass (200, 201, 202, 203, 204, 866). Supporting documents required as per application guidelines.

overview: Sirius College offers scholarships to students demonstrating special talents. This category recognizes students with diverse skills, interests and attributes beyond traditional academic achievement.
description: Sirius College supports students with a wide range of skills, interests and attributes. Scholarships are awarded to recognize special talents and capabilities in areas not covered by academic excellence or community service categories.
eligibility_criteria: Students demonstrating special talents, skills or attributes in areas such as arts, music, sports or other specialized areas of excellence.

overview: St John's College Preston offers General Excellence Scholarships for students seeking enrolment. The scholarship recognises academic achievement and excellence. Application details and requirements are available on the school's dedicated scholarships page.
description: The General Excellence Scholarships are merit-based scholarships offered by St John's College Preston to support students who demonstrate academic excellence. These scholarships are designed to provide financial assistance to high-achieving students. The school has a dedicated scholarship page for applicants providing information about the program and application process.
eligibility_criteria: Students applying for the General Excellence Scholarship must meet academic merit criteria as determined by the school. Specific academic requirements, year level restrictions, and other eligibility details are available through the school's scholarship application page.
special_conditions: Non-refundable $50 application fee applies

overview: Edmund Rice Bursary (means-tested for Year 7) and Choral Scholarships (Year 5, tied to St Patrick's Cathedral Choir); applications closed for 2026; merit and financial need basis.
Edmund Rice Bursary (means-tested for Year 7) and Choral Scholarships (Year 5, tied to St Patrick's Cathedral Choir); applications closed for 2026; merit and financial need basis.
Leonardian Scholarships are awarded to exceptional students who demonstrate outstanding academic achievement and potential.
description: Leonardian Scholarships recognise exceptional academic achievement and are part of the College's commitment to supporting talented students. These scholarships are awarded to students whose life circumstances present barriers to success.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7-11 who demonstrate exceptional academic achievement and potential.

overview: Global Citizen Scholarships support students who demonstrate financial need and a commitment to service and global awareness.
description: Global Citizen Scholarships are awarded to students whose life circumstances present barriers to success. These life-changing scholarships open doors for students and equip them to create change for themselves, their families, their communities and their world.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering Years 7-11 who demonstrate financial need and commitment to service and global values.

overview: Indigenous Scholarships are awarded to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander students who seek to access an independent school education.
description: Indigenous Scholarships may be awarded to Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander prospective students entering Years 7 to 11. The College has close relationships with the Warruwi community and Melbourne Indigenous Transition School. These life-changing scholarships open doors for students and equip them to create change for themselves, their families, their communities and their world.
eligibility_criteria: Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander students entering Years 7-11.
special_conditions: Applicants must be Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander.

overview: ASPIRE Scholarships are awarded to students facing ongoing difficult financial, family, or other life circumstances which have prevented them from achieving their best.
description: These ASPIRE scholarships are awarded to students who face ongoing difficult financial, family, or other life circumstances which have prevented them from achieving their best. The program is supported by donations to the St Leonard's College ASPIRE Scholarship program, enabling worthy young people the opportunity to receive an education for life.
eligibility_criteria: Students facing financial, family, or other challenging life circumstances that present barriers to education. Available for Years 7-11.
special_conditions: Applicants must demonstrate financial need or difficult family/life circumstances.

school_name: St Margaret’s Berwick Grammar - Berwick Grammar School
overview: The St Margaret's Berwick Grammar Bursary Scheme is designed to provide the broadest possible access to the school for families experiencing financial hardship. The scheme is administered through Edstart to ensure financial privacy and assess bursary values based on individual family circumstances.
description: The Bursary Scheme provides financial assistance to families who require support to access a St Margaret's Berwick Grammar education. Financial information is submitted through the Edstart portal and is reviewed confidentially by Edstart rather than the school, ensuring complete financial privacy. The school then assesses applications considering multiple factors including place availability, alumni status, siblings, student interview and student performance. Bursaries are reviewed in full every 3 years or when there is a significant change in family circumstances such as a sibling joining the school. The value of bursary assistance varies based on individual family circumstances as determined by Edstart assessment.
eligibility_criteria: Open to families experiencing financial hardship seeking admission to St Margaret's Berwick Grammar. Applications are assessed based on financial need, place availability, alumni status, siblings, student interview and student performance.
special_conditions: Bursary discounts apply to tuition fee component only. Students receiving a bursary must remain at the school until end of Year 12 in accordance with the terms of the bursary agreement.

school_name: St Margaret’s Berwick Grammar - Berwick Grammar School
overview: St Margaret's Berwick Grammar offers a comprehensive scholarship program based on their educational philosophy of the 4 As: Academics, Athleticism, Artistry and Adventure. The school awards scholarships to talented individuals across a range of disciplines to support exceptional students.
description: St Margaret's Berwick Grammar has designed a scholarship program reflecting their 4 As framework to identify and celebrate talented students across multiple areas: Academics (pursuing academic achievement and excellence), Athleticism (pursuing a healthy body to complement a healthy mind), Artistry (pursuing and fostering creative flair), and Adventure (fueling growth and experiences outside the classroom). Scholarships are awarded as part remission of fees to students who meet the standards set out for each individual scholarship category. Scholarship values vary and the school has a strong commitment to assisting students who would greatly benefit from a St Margaret's education. Students receiving scholarships must remain at the school until the end of Year 12 in accordance with the terms of the Scholarship Agreement.
eligibility_criteria: Students seeking admission to St Margaret's Berwick Grammar who demonstrate excellence in one or more of the 4 As categories (Academics, Athleticism, Artistry, Adventure). Scholarships apply to tuition fee component only. Specific eligibility criteria for each scholarship category available when 2026 applications open.
special_conditions: Scholarship applications open in March 2026 and exams are sat in May 2026. Students receiving scholarships must remain at the school until end of Year 12.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Drouin Campus
overview: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School offers annual Academic Scholarships recognizing outstanding achievement in academic work. These scholarships are awarded to students who achieve excellent results in external EduTest scholarship examinations and demonstrate exceptional commitment to their learning.
description: The Academic Scholarship is awarded to students who achieve excellent results in the external EduTest scholarship examinations. Recipients are hard-working students who are intentional about their learning and strive to extend their minds. Scholarship recipients understand and value a St Paul's education, one that will inspire and challenge them to succeed in their learning and develop strong character. Academic Scholarships can significantly offset tuition fees and are designed to support dedicated and capable students who are committed to their studies and have a genuine desire to achieve their potential.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering secondary in 2027 or later. Must be capable students with genuine commitment to learning. No specific residency requirements stated. Open to students who demonstrate exceptional academic potential and can make a valuable contribution to the school community.
special_conditions: Offered to students who achieve excellent results in external EduTest scholarship examinations. Applications for secondary students in 2027 now being accepted.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Drouin Campus
overview: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School offers Personal Excellence Scholarships recognizing students with exceptional passion and commitment to pursuing their purpose. These scholarships support students who demonstrate outstanding potential in areas such as sport, performing arts, visual arts, STEM, business/entrepreneurship, community service, leadership, or environmental awareness.
description: The Personal Excellence Scholarship is offered to students who demonstrate their potential to be outstanding contributors in their chosen interest/discipline fields such as sport, performing arts, visual arts, STEM, business/entrepreneurship, community service/leadership and environmental awareness. Recipients are hard-working students of excellent character who are keen to make a valuable contribution to the school community. These scholarships recognize and empower students with exceptional passion and commitment to pursuing their purpose. Personal Excellence Scholarships can significantly offset tuition fees for qualified students.
eligibility_criteria: Students entering secondary in 2027 or later. Must demonstrate exceptional potential in chosen discipline (sport, performing arts, visual arts, STEM, business/entrepreneurship, community service/leadership, or environmental awareness). Must be of excellent character and show commitment to contributing to the school community. No specific residency requirements stated.
special_conditions: Applications for secondary students in 2027 now being accepted. Disciplines include sport, performing arts, visual arts, STEM, business/entrepreneurship, community service/leadership, and environmental awareness.

school_name: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School - Drouin Campus
overview: St Paul's Anglican Grammar School offers Foundation Scholarships through their Education Support Program. This program provides financial assistance to capable and dedicated students who would benefit from attending St Paul's but may be unable to do so without assistance due to financial or other circumstances.
description: The Foundation Scholarship is part of St Paul's Education Support Program which provides a unique opportunity to invite applications from dedicated and capable students who would benefit from attending St Paul's, but for financial or other circumstances may be unable to do so without assistance. Such students will have a genuine desire to achieve their potential, be committed to their studies, be a positive influence on those around them and have the desire to participate in an environment where learning is valued and respected. These scholarships can significantly offset tuition fees and are designed to make a St Paul's education accessible to talented students from diverse backgrounds.
eligibility_criteria: Dedicated and capable students who would benefit from a St Paul's education. Must have genuine desire to achieve their potential. Must be committed to their studies and be a positive influence on others. Must have desire to participate in a learning-focused environment. Financial need or other circumstances must prevent attendance without assistance. No specific year level restrictions stated.
special_conditions: Part of Education Support Program. Designed for students facing financial or other circumstances preventing attendance.

overview: Awarded to students with a proven record of exceptional academic achievement in three or more disciplines. This merit-based scholarship recognizes outstanding academic performance and potential for continued excellence.
description: The Academic Scholarship is offered to students demonstrating exceptional academic achievement across multiple disciplines. It recognizes students with proven record of achievement in three or more disciplines, consistent achievement in other disciplines, and the potential to continue to achieve outstanding academic results. Scholarships are awarded as a percentage reduction in tuition fees (25-75%) for the duration of the student's time at the College until completion of Year 12, subject to satisfactory progress.
eligibility_criteria: Students with proven record of exceptional academic achievement in three or more disciplines, consistent achievement in other disciplines, and potential to continue to achieve outstanding academic results. Available for current and non-current students. All scholarships valid from year of award until completion of Year 12.
special_conditions: Student progress assessed on ongoing basis and formally reviewed at end of Year 6 and Year 9. Scholarship students assigned mentor to support learning journey.

overview: Awarded to students who have demonstrated strengths, interests and achievements in a broad range of activities including leadership, sport, performing and visual arts, academic performance, and community service.
description: The All-Rounder Scholarship recognizes students with demonstrated strengths and achievements across multiple domains. This may include a combination of consistent academic achievement, sporting prowess, musical ability, school leadership, extra-curricular pursuits, and community involvement. Students are evaluated on their holistic contributions to College life and potential to continue these diverse achievements. Scholarships are awarded as a percentage reduction in tuition fees (25-75%) for the duration of the student's time at the College until completion of Year 12, subject to satisfactory progress.
eligibility_criteria: Students who have demonstrated strengths, interests and achievements in a broad range of activities that may include leadership, sport, the performing and visual arts, academic performance or community service. Available for current and non-current students. All scholarships valid from year of award until completion of Year 12.
special_conditions: Student progress assessed on ongoing basis and formally reviewed at end of Year 6 and Year 9. Scholarship students assigned mentor to support learning journey. Participation in College events and activities expected.

overview: Financial assistance component of the scholarship program that provides additional support for families with demonstrated financial need and relevant personal circumstances seeking opportunity of traditional Catholic education.
description: Financial Assistance is available as part of the College's scholarship program. Families may apply for financial assistance as part of their scholarship application. This component seeks to provide additional support for families with demonstrated financial need and relevant personal circumstances seeking the opportunity of a traditional Catholic education. The assistance can be combined with merit-based scholarships to provide comprehensive financial support.
eligibility_criteria: Families with demonstrated financial need and relevant personal circumstances seeking opportunity of traditional Catholic education. Applicants must complete application form providing requested information. Available for current and non-current students.

overview: The Young Carer Bursary Program supports young carers to continue with their education by providing financial assistance. The program offers 1000 bursaries of $3000 each year to assist with education needs and resources.
description: This program is designed to support young carers who balance caring responsibilities with their education. It provides financial assistance of $3000 per year to help with education-related expenses and resources. The bursary recognises the challenges faced by young carers and aims to ensure they can access the support they need to continue their studies successfully.
eligibility_criteria: Must be a young carer recognised by the Young Carers Network. Eligible young carers can apply for the bursary by logging into their account and completing an online application form.
special_conditions: Applicants must be registered with the Young Carers Network. Must login to account to complete online application.

overview: The Department of Education and Training Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund helps ensure that no student at Wodonga Middle Years College misses out on important educational and fun activities. This program provides financial assistance to eligible families to support student participation in camps, sports and excursions.
description: The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is a Victorian government program that assists eligible families with the cost of school camps, sports and excursions. The fund ensures all students have access to the world of learning opportunities that exist beyond the classroom. Wodonga Middle Years College administers this fund to support student participation in important educational experiences.
eligibility_criteria: Eligibility is based on family circumstances and financial need. Families receiving eligible Centrelink payments or holding a Veterans Affairs Gold Card may be eligible for assistance.
special_conditions: This is a government program administered by Victorian government schools. Students must be attending Wodonga Middle Years College.

overview: A bursary program offering financial assistance to students who are young carers. The program provides $3000 per year to assist with education needs and resources for young carers at the college.
description: The Young Carer Bursary Program offers financial support to students who have caring responsibilities. The program distributes 1000 bursaries of $3000 each year to assist with education needs and resources. This initiative recognises and supports young carers attending Wodonga Middle Years College by reducing financial barriers to their education.
eligibility_criteria: Students who are young carers (having caring responsibilities for family members or relatives).
special_conditions: Applicant must be a young carer with documented caring responsibilities.

overview: A government-funded program that ensures no student misses out on important educational and fun activities. The Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) helps make these opportunities accessible to all students at Wodonga Middle Years College.
description: The Department of Education and Training Camps, Sports and Excursions Fund (CSEF) is administered by Wodonga Middle Years College to ensure equitable access to camps, sports, and excursions. The program helps eligible families with the costs associated with these educational activities, ensuring that financial hardship is not a barrier to student participation in important enrichment opportunities beyond the classroom.
eligibility_criteria: Students from eligible families meeting financial hardship criteria as determined by the Department of Education and Training.
special_conditions: Eligibility based on financial need criteria determined by the Department of Education and Training.

school_name: Wodonga Middle Years College-Flying Fruit Fly Circus School
overview: The Noel Tovey Scholarship Fund helps to ensure there is no financial barrier to enrolment at the Flying Fruit Fly Circus. This scholarship assists talented young performers who face financial hardship that could prevent them from attending the school.
description: The Noel Tovey Scholarship Fund is designed to help ensure there is no financial barrier to enrolment at the Flying Fruit Fly Circus School. Being a young artist at the Flying Fruit Fly Circus is a full-time job, with students attending school for 30 hours a week and training between 9 and 20 hours per week. For some talented young performers from across Australia who audition for a place, financial hardship can stand in the way of an extraordinary opportunity. Scholarships can help cover tuition, travel, training, and the costs of daily life.
eligibility_criteria: Students who have auditioned and been offered a place at the Flying Fruit Fly Circus School and demonstrate financial hardship that would otherwise prevent them from attending.
special_conditions: Must have auditioned and been offered a place at the Flying Fruit Fly Circus School

school_name: Wodonga Middle Years College-Flying Fruit Fly Circus School
overview: The Neil McDermott Scholarship Fund provides a 50% contribution towards the fees of a deserving student who may otherwise struggle to attend the Flying Fruit Fly Circus. The fund honours the memory of Neil McDermott, a valued teacher and passionate supporter of the Circus.
description: The Neil McDermott Scholarship Fund provides a 50% contribution towards the fees of a deserving student who may otherwise struggle to attend the Flying Fruit Fly Circus. The Scholarship Fund has been created with permission of Neil McDermott's family to honour his memory and his legacy as a valued teacher, passionate supporter and wonderful friend of the Circus. As a teacher at the school, Neil nurtured and mentored multiple generations of students and contributed to many aspects of the circus work.
eligibility_criteria: Students who have been offered a place at the Flying Fruit Fly Circus School and demonstrate financial need that would otherwise prevent them from attending.
special_conditions: Must have auditioned and been offered a place at the Flying Fruit Fly Circus School

special_conditions: Student must be a young carer with caregiving responsibilities
